49:   ##
50:   # Requires an optional runtime dependency
51:   # @raise [LLM::DependencyError]
52:   #  When the dependency cannot be loaded
53:   def self.require(name)
54:     super
55:   rescue ::LoadError
56:     names = {"xchan" => "xchan.rb", "net/http/persistent" => "net-http-persistent"}
57:     name = names[name] || name
58:     raise LLM::LoadError,
59:       "#{name} is an optional runtime dependency but it does not appear to be installed. " \
60:       "Consider 'gem install #{name}', adding '#{name}' to your Gemfile or " \
61:       "opting out of the functionality provided by '#{name}'"
62:   end

64:   ##
65:   # @param [Symbol, LLM::Provider] llm
66:   #  The name of a provider, or an instance of LLM::Provider
67:   # @return [LLM::Object]
68:   def self.registry_for(llm)
69:     lock(:registry) do
70:       name = Symbol === llm ? llm : llm.name
71:       @registry[name] ||= Registry.for(name)
72:     end
73:   end

79:   # @return [Class]
80:   #  Returns a class that responds to `dump` and `load`
81:   def json
82:     @json ||= JSONAdapter::JSON
83:   end

85:   ##
86:   # Sets the JSON adapter used by the library
87:   # @note
88:   #  This should be set once from the main thread when your program starts.
89:   #  Defaults to {LLM::JSONAdapter::JSON LLM::JSONAdapter::JSON}.
90:   # @param [Class, String, Symbol] adapter
91:   #  A JSON adapter class or its name
92:   # @return [void]
93:   def json=(adapter)
94:     @json = case adapter.to_s
95:     when "JSON", "json" then JSONAdapter::JSON
96:     when "Oj", "oj" then JSONAdapter::Oj
97:     when "Yajl", "yajl" then JSONAdapter::Yajl
98:     else
99:       is_class = Class === adapter
100:       is_subclass = is_class && adapter.ancestors.include?(LLM::JSONAdapter)
101:       if is_subclass
102:         adapter
103:       else
104:         raise TypeError, "Adapter must be a subclass of LLM::JSONAdapter"
105:       end
106:     end
107:   end

202:   # @option http [Hash<String, String>] :headers
203:   #  Extra HTTP headers (e.g., Authorization)
204:   # @option http [Integer, nil] :timeout
205:   #  Request timeout in seconds
206:   # @option http [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] :transport
207:   #  Optional transport override

221:   #       schema.object(command: schema.string.required)
222:   #     end
223:   #     fn.define do |command:|

225:   #     end
226:   #   end
227:   # @param [Symbol] key The function name / key
228:   # @param [Proc] b The block to define the function

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # The Provider class represents an abstract class for
5: # LLM (Language Model) providers.
6: #
7: # @abstract
8: class LLM::Provider
9:   include LLM::Transport::Execution
10: 
11:   ##
12:   # @param [String, nil] key
13:   #  The secret key for authentication
14:   # @param [String] host
15:   #  The host address of the LLM provider
16:   # @param [Integer] port
17:   #  The port number
18:   # @param [Integer] timeout
19:   #  The number of seconds to wait for a response
20:   # @param [Boolean] ssl
21:   #  Whether to use SSL for the connection
22:   # @param [String] base_path
23:   #  Optional base path prefix for HTTP API routes.
24:   # @param [Boolean] persistent
25:   #  Whether to use a persistent connection.
26:   #  Requires the net-http-persistent gem.
27:   # @param [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] transport
28:   #  Optional override with any {LLM::Transport} instance or subclass.
29:   def initialize(key:, host:, port: 443, timeout: 60, ssl: true, base_path: "", persistent: false, transport: nil)
30:     @key = key
31:     @host = host
32:     @port = port
33:     @timeout = timeout
34:     @ssl = ssl
35:     @base_path = LLM::Utils.normalize_base_path(base_path)
36:     @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
37:     @headers = {"User-Agent" => "llm.rb v#{LLM::VERSION}"}
38:     @transport = LLM::Transport::Utils.resolve_transport(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:, transport:, persistent:)
39:     @monitor = Monitor.new
40:   end
41: 
42:   ##
43:   # Returns an inspection of the provider object
44:   # @return [String]
45:   # @note The secret key is redacted in inspect for security reasons
46:   def inspect
47:     "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @key=[REDACTED] @transport=#{transport.inspect} @tracer=#{tracer.inspect}>"
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
52:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
53:   # @return [Symbol]
54:   #  Returns the provider's name
55:   def name
56:     raise NotImplementedError
57:   end
58: 
59:   ##
60:   # Provides an embedding
61:   # @param [String, Array<String>] input
62:   #  The input to embed
63:   # @param [String] model
64:   #  The embedding model to use
65:   # @param [Hash] params
66:   #  Other embedding parameters
67:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
68:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
69:   # @return [LLM::Response]
70:   def embed(input, model: nil, **params)
71:     raise NotImplementedError
72:   end
73: 
74:   ##
75:   # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
76:   # @example
77:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
78:   #   messages = [{role: "system", content: "Your task is to answer all of my questions"}]
79:   #   res = llm.complete("5 + 2 ?", messages:)
80:   #   print "[#{res.messages[0].role}]", res.messages[0].content, "\n"
81:   # @param [String] prompt
82:   #  The input prompt to be completed
83:   # @param [Hash] params
84:   #  The parameters to maintain throughout the conversation.
85:   #  Any parameter the provider supports can be included and
86:   #  not only those listed here.
87:   # @option params [Symbol] :role Defaults to the provider's default role
88:   # @option params [String] :model Defaults to the provider's default model
89:   # @option params [#to_json, nil] :schema Defaults to nil
90:   # @option params [Array<LLM::Function>, nil] :tools Defaults to nil
91:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
92:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
93:   # @return [LLM::Response]
94:   def complete(prompt, params = {})
95:     raise NotImplementedError
96:   end
97: 
98:   ##
99:   # Starts a new chat powered by the chat completions API
100:   # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
101:   # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
102:   # @return [LLM::Context]
103:   def chat(prompt, params = {})
104:     role = params.delete(:role)
105:     LLM::Context.new(self, params).talk(prompt, role:)
106:   end
107: 
108:   ##
109:   # Starts a new chat powered by the responses API
110:   # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
111:   # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
112:   # @raise (see LLM::Provider#complete)
113:   # @return [LLM::Context]
114:   def respond(prompt, params = {})
115:     role = params.delete(:role)
116:     LLM::Context.new(self, params).respond(prompt, role:)
117:   end
118: 
119:   ##
120:   # @note
121:   # Compared to the chat completions API, the responses API
122:   # can require less bandwidth on each turn, maintain state
123:   # server-side, and produce faster responses.
124:   # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Responses]
125:   #  Returns an interface to the responses API
126:   def responses
127:     raise NotImplementedError
128:   end
129: 
130:   ##
131:   # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Images, LLM::Google::Images]
132:   #  Returns an interface to the images API
133:   def images
134:     raise NotImplementedError
135:   end
136: 
137:   ##
138:   # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Audio]
139:   #  Returns an interface to the audio API
140:   def audio
141:     raise NotImplementedError
142:   end
143: 
144:   ##
145:   # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Files]
146:   #  Returns an interface to the files API
147:   def files
148:     raise NotImplementedError
149:   end
150: 
151:   ##
152:   # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Models]
153:   #  Returns an interface to the models API
154:   def models
155:     raise NotImplementedError
156:   end
157: 
158:   ##
159:   # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Moderations]
160:   #  Returns an interface to the moderations API
161:   def moderations
162:     raise NotImplementedError
163:   end
164: 
165:   ##
166:   # @return [LLM::OpenAI::VectorStore]
167:   #  Returns an interface to the vector stores API
168:   def vector_stores
169:     raise NotImplementedError
170:   end
171: 
172:   ##
173:   # @return [String]
174:   #  Returns the role of the assistant in the conversation.
175:   #  Usually "assistant" or "model"
176:   def assistant_role
177:     raise NotImplementedError
178:   end
179: 
180:   ##
181:   # @return [String]
182:   #  Returns the default model for chat completions
183:   def default_model
184:     raise NotImplementedError
185:   end
186: 
187:   ##
188:   # Returns an object that can generate a JSON schema
189:   # @return [LLM::Schema]
190:   def schema
191:     LLM::Schema.new
192:   end
193: 
194:   ##
195:   # Add one or more headers to all requests
196:   # @example
197:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
198:   #   llm.with(headers: {"OpenAI-Organization" => ENV["ORG"]})
199:   #   llm.with(headers: {"OpenAI-Project" => ENV["PROJECT"]})
200:   # @param [Hash<String,String>] headers
201:   #  One or more headers
202:   # @return [LLM::Provider]
203:   #  Returns self
204:   def with(headers:)
205:     lock do
206:       tap { @headers.merge!(headers) }
207:     end
208:   end
209: 
210:   ##
211:   # @note
212:   #  This method might be outdated, and the {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool}
213:   #  method can be used if a tool is not found here.
214:   # Returns all known tools provided by a provider.
215:   # @return [String => LLM::ServerTool]
216:   def server_tools
217:     {}
218:   end
219: 
220:   ##
221:   # @note
222:   #   OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini provide platform-tools for things
223:   #   like web search, and more.
224:   # Returns a tool provided by a provider.
225:   # @example
226:   #   llm   = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
227:   #   tools = [llm.server_tool(:web_search)]
228:   #   res   = llm.responses.create("Summarize today's news", tools:)
229:   #   print res.output_text, "\n"
230:   # @param [String, Symbol] name The name of the tool
231:   # @param [Hash] options Configuration options for the tool
232:   # @return [LLM::ServerTool]
233:   def server_tool(name, options = {})
234:     LLM::ServerTool.new(name, options, self)
235:   end
236: 
237:   ##
238:   # Provides a web search capability
239:   # @param [String] query The search query
240:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
241:   #  When the method is not implemented by a subclass
242:   # @return [LLM::Response]
243:   def web_search(query:)
244:     raise NotImplementedError
245:   end
246: 
247:   ##
248:   # @return [Symbol]
249:   def user_role
250:     :user
251:   end
252: 
253:   ##
254:   # @return [Symbol]
255:   def system_role
256:     :system
257:   end
258: 
259:   ##
260:   # @return [Symbol]
261:   def developer_role
262:     :developer
263:   end
264: 
265:   ##
266:   # @return [Symbol]
267:   def tool_role
268:     :tool
269:   end
270: 
271:   ##
272:   # @return [LLM::Tracer]
273:   #  Returns the current scoped tracer override or provider default tracer
274:   def tracer
275:     weakmap[self] || @tracer || LLM::Tracer::Null.new(self)
276:   end
277: 
278:   ##
279:   # Set the provider's default tracer
280:   # This tracer is shared by the provider instance and becomes the fallback
281:   # whenever no scoped override is active.
282:   # @example
283:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
284:   #   llm.tracer = LLM::Tracer::Logger.new(llm, path: "/path/to/log.txt")
285:   # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
286:   #  A tracer
287:   # @return [void]
288:   def tracer=(tracer)
289:     @tracer = tracer
290:   end
291: 
292:   ##
293:   # Override the tracer for the current fiber while the block runs.
294:   # This is useful when you want per-request or per-turn tracing without
295:   # replacing the provider's default tracer.
296:   # @example
297:   #   llm.with_tracer(LLM::Tracer::Logger.new(llm, io: $stdout)) do
298:   #     llm.complete("hello", model: "gpt-5.4-mini")
299:   #   end
300:   # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
301:   # @yield
302:   # @return [Object]
303:   def with_tracer(tracer)
304:     had_override = weakmap.key?(self)
305:     previous = weakmap[self]
306:     weakmap[self] = tracer
307:     yield
308:   ensure
309:     if had_override
310:       weakmap[self] = previous
311:     elsif weakmap.respond_to?(:delete)
312:       weakmap.delete(self)
313:     else
314:       weakmap[self] = nil
315:     end
316:   end
317: 
318:   ##
319:   # Interrupt the active request, if any.
320:   # @param [Fiber] owner
321:   # @return [nil]
322:   def interrupt!(owner)
323:     transport.interrupt!(owner)
324:   end
325:   alias_method :cancel!, :interrupt!
326: 
327:   ##
328:   # Returns the current request owner used by the transport.
329:   # @return [Object]
330:   # @api private
331:   def request_owner
332:     transport.request_owner
333:   end
334: 
335:   ##
336:   # @param [Object] stream
337:   # @return [Boolean]
338:   def streamable?(stream)
339:     LLM::Stream === stream || stream.respond_to?(:<<)
340:   end
341: 
342:   ##
343:   # @return [Boolean]
344:   #  Returns true when an API key is configured
345:   def key?
346:     @key != nil && @key.to_s.strip.size > 0
347:   end

349:   private

351:   def path(suffix)
352:     return suffix if @base_path.empty?
353:     "#{@base_path}#{suffix}"
354:   end

356:   attr_reader :base_uri, :host, :port, :timeout, :ssl, :transport

361:   #  (see LLM::Provider#complete)
362:   def headers

364:   end

369:   # @raise [NotImplementedError]
370:   #  (see LLM::Provider#complete)
371:   def error_handler
372:     raise NotImplementedError
373:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # The {LLM::Registry LLM::Registry} class provides a small API over
5: # provider model data. It exposes model metadata such as pricing,
6: # capabilities, modalities, and limits from the registry files
7: # stored under `data/`. The data is provided by https://models.dev
8: # and shipped with llm.rb.
9: class LLM::Registry
10:   @root = File.join(__dir__, "..", "..")
11: 
12:   ##
13:   # @raise [LLM::Error]
14:   #  Might raise an error
15:   # @param [Symbol]
16:   #  A provider name
17:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
18:   def self.for(name)
19:     path = File.join @root, "data", "#{name}.json"
20:     if File.file?(path)
21:       new LLM.json.load(File.binread(path))
22:     else
23:       raise LLM::NoSuchRegistryError, "no registry found for #{name}"
24:     end
25:   end
26: 
27:   ##
28:   # @param [Hash] blob
29:   #  A model registry
30:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
31:   def initialize(blob)
32:     @registry = LLM::Object.from(blob)
33:     @models = @registry.models
34:   end
35: 
36:   ##
37:   # @return [LLM::Object]
38:   #  Returns model costs
39:   def cost(model:)
40:     lookup(model:).cost
41:   end
42: 
43:   ##
44:   # @return [LLM::Object]
45:   #  Returns model modalities
46:   def modalities(model:)
47:     lookup(model:).modalities
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @return [LLM::Object]
52:   #  Returns model limits such as the context window size
53:   def limit(model:)
54:     lookup(model:).limit
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def lookup(model:)
60:     if @models.key?(model)
61:       @models[model]
62:     else
63:       patterns = {/-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ => "", /\A(gpt-.*)-\d{4}$/ => "\\1"}
64:       fallback = find_map(patterns) { model.dup.sub!(_1, _2) } || "none"
65:       if @models.key?(fallback)
66:         @models[fallback]
67:       else
68:         raise LLM::NoSuchModelError, "no such model: #{model} (fallback: #{fallback})"
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   ##
74:   # Similar to #{find} but returns the block's return value
75:   # @return [Object, nil]
76:   def find_map(pair)
77:     result = nil
78:     pair.each_pair { break if result = yield(_1, _2) }
79:     result
80:   end
81: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::Transport LLM::Transport} class defines the execution
6:   # interface used by {LLM::Provider}.
7:   #
8:   # Custom transports can subclass this class and override {#request} to
9:   # execute provider requests without changing request adapters or
10:   # response adapters.
11:   #
12:   # Providers should construct {LLM::Transport::Request} objects before
13:   # delegating to a transport. Custom transports can execute those
14:   # requests directly, or transform them into backend-specific request
15:   # objects before execution.
16:   #
17:   # Only {#request} is required. The remaining methods are optional hooks
18:   # for features such as interruption, request ownership, or persistence,
19:   # and only need to be implemented when the underlying adapter can
20:   # support them.
21:   #
22:   # Returned responses should implement the
23:   # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} interface. In
24:   # practice this can mean adapting another client's response object so
25:   # existing provider execution, response adapters, and error handlers
26:   # can rely on one normalized response contract instead of
27:   # transport-specific classes.
28:   class Transport
29:     require_relative "transport/request"
30:     require_relative "transport/response"
31:     require_relative "transport/utils"
32:     require_relative "transport/stream_decoder"
33:     require_relative "transport/net_http_adapter"
34:     require_relative "transport/http"
35:     require_relative "transport/persistent_http"
36:     require_relative "transport/curb"
37:     require_relative "transport/execution"
38: 
39:     ##
40:     # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP transport class.
41:     # @return [Class]
42:     def self.net_http
43:       HTTP
44:     end
45: 
46:     ##
47:     # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP::Persistent transport class.
48:     # @return [Class]
49:     def self.net_http_persistent
50:       PersistentHTTP
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # Returns the optional libcurl (curb) transport class.
55:     # Requires the `curb` gem.
56:     # @return [Class]
57:     def self.curb
58:       Curb
59:     end
60: 
61:     ##
62:     # Performs a request through the transport.
63:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
64:     # @param [Object] owner
65:     # @param [LLM::Object, nil] stream
66:     # @yieldparam [LLM::Transport::Response] response
67:     # @return [Object]
68:     def request(request, owner:, stream: nil, &)
69:       raise NotImplementedError
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # Returns the current request owner.
74:     # @return [Object]
75:     def request_owner
76:       return Fiber.current unless defined?(::Async)
77:       Async::Task.current? ? Async::Task.current : Fiber.current
78:     end
79: 
80:     ##
81:     # Returns the exception classes that indicate an interrupted request.
82:     # @return [Array<Class<Exception>>]
83:     def interrupt_errors
84:       []
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # Interrupt an active request, if any.
89:     # @param [Object] owner
90:     # @return [nil]
91:     def interrupt!(owner)
92:       raise NotImplementedError
93:     end
94: 
95:     ##
96:     # Returns whether an execution owner was interrupted.
97:     # @param [Object] owner
98:     # @return [Boolean, nil]
99:     def interrupted?(owner)
100:       nil
101:     end
102: 
103:     ##
104:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
105:     # @param [IO] io
106:     # @return [void]
107:     def set_body_stream(request, io)
108:       request.body_stream = io
109:       request["transfer-encoding"] = "chunked" unless request["content-length"]
110:     end
111:   end
112: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Response LLM::Response} is the normalized base shape for
6:   # provider and endpoint responses in llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # Provider calls return an instance of this class, then extend it
9:   # with provider-, endpoint-, or context-specific modules so response
10:   # handling can share one common surface without flattening away
11:   # specialized behavior.
12:   #
13:   # The normalized response keeps the transport response available
14:   # through {#res}. When the default net/http transport is in use,
15:   # {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP
16:   # LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP} keeps the
17:   # original {Net::HTTPResponse Net::HTTPResponse} available through
18:   # its own {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP#res #res}.
19:   class Response
20:     require "json"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # Returns the HTTP response
24:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
25:     attr_reader :res
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
29:     #  HTTP response
30:     # @return [LLM::Response]
31:     #  Returns an instance of LLM::Response
32:     def initialize(res)
33:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
34:     end
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # Returns the response body
38:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
39:     #  Returns an LLM::Object when the response body is JSON,
40:     #  otherwise returns a raw string.
41:     def body
42:       @res.body
43:     end
44: 
45:     ##
46:     # Returns an inspection of the response object
47:     # @return [String]
48:     def inspect
49:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @body=#{body.inspect} @res=#{@res.inspect}>"
50:     end
51: 
52:     ##
53:     # Returns true if the response is successful
54:     # @return [Boolean]
55:     def ok?
56:       @res.success?
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Returns true if the response is from the Files API
61:     # @return [Boolean]
62:     def file?
63:       false
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     def method_missing(m, *args, **kwargs, &b)
69:       if LLM::Object === body
70:         body.respond_to?(m) ? body[m.to_s] : super
71:       else
72:         super
73:       end
74:     end
75: 
76:     def respond_to_missing?(m, include_private = false)
77:       if LLM::Object === body
78:         body.respond_to?(m)
79:       else
80:         false
81:       end
82:     end
83:   end
84: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # Internal request execution methods for {LLM::Provider}.
6:   #
7:   # This module handles provider-side transport execution, response
8:   # parsing, streaming, and request body setup.
9:   #
10:   # @api private
11:   module Execution
12:     private
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Executes a HTTP request
16:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
17:     #  The request to send
18:     # @param [Proc] b
19:     #  A block to yield the response to (optional)
20:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
21:     #  The response from the server
22:     # @raise [LLM::Error::Unauthorized]
23:     #  When authentication fails
24:     # @raise [LLM::Error::RateLimit]
25:     #  When the rate limit is exceeded
26:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
27:     #  When any other unsuccessful status code is returned
28:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
29:     #  When there is a network error at the operating system level
30:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
31:     def execute(request:, operation:, stream: nil, stream_parser: self.stream_parser, model: nil, inputs: nil, &b)
32:       stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
33:       owner = transport.request_owner
34:       tracer = self.tracer
35:       span = tracer.on_request_start(operation:, model:, inputs:)
36:       res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
37:       res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
38:       [handle_response(res, tracer, span), span, tracer]
39:     rescue *transport.interrupt_errors
40:       raise LLM::Interrupt, "request interrupted" if transport.interrupted?(owner)
41:       raise
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # Handles the response from a request
46:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
47:     #  The response to handle
48:     # @param [Object, nil] span
49:     #  The span
50:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
51:     def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
52:       res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
53:       res
54:     end
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Parse a HTTP response
58:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
59:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
60:     def parse_response(res)
61:       case res["content-type"]
62:       when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
63:       else res.body
64:       end
65:     end
66:   end
67: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Transport::Request LLM::Transport::Request} defines the
6:   # normalized request interface expected by transports.
7:   #
8:   # Providers build request objects through this class, then hand them
9:   # to a transport for execution without depending on any specific HTTP
10:   # client library.
11:   class Request
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     attr_accessor :body
15: 
16:     ##
17:     # @return [IO, nil]
18:     attr_accessor :body_stream
19: 
20:     ##
21:     # @return [String]
22:     attr_reader :method
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @return [String]
26:     attr_reader :path
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @return [Hash]
30:     attr_reader :headers
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # @param [String] path
34:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
35:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
36:     def self.get(path, headers = nil)
37:       new("GET", path, headers)
38:     end
39: 
40:     ##
41:     # @param [String] path
42:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
43:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
44:     def self.post(path, headers = nil)
45:       new("POST", path, headers)
46:     end
47: 
48:     ##
49:     # @param [String] path
50:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
51:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
52:     def self.put(path, headers = nil)
53:       new("PUT", path, headers)
54:     end
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # @param [String] path
58:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
59:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
60:     def self.patch(path, headers = nil)
61:       new("PATCH", path, headers)
62:     end
63: 
64:     ##
65:     # @param [String] path
66:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
67:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
68:     def self.delete(path, headers = nil)
69:       new("DELETE", path, headers)
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @param [String] method
74:     # @param [String] path
75:     # @param [Hash, nil] headers
76:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Request]
77:     def initialize(method, path, headers = nil)
78:       @method = method.to_s.upcase
79:       @path = path.to_s
80:       @headers = {}
81:       (headers || {}).each { self[_1] = _2 }
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # @param [String] key
86:     # @return [String, nil]
87:     def [](key)
88:       @headers[normalize_header(key)]
89:     end
90: 
91:     ##
92:     # @param [String] key
93:     # @param [Object] value
94:     # @return [String]
95:     def []=(key, value)
96:       @headers[normalize_header(key)] = value.to_s
97:     end
98: 
99:     ##
100:     # @yieldparam [String] key
101:     # @yieldparam [String] value
102:     # @return [Hash]
103:     def each_header(&block)
104:       @headers.each(&block)
105:     end
106: 
107:     ##
108:     # @return [String]
109:     def inspect
110:       "#<#{self.class.name}:0x#{object_id.to_s(16)}" \
111:         " @method=#{@method} @path=#{@path}" \
112:         " @headers=#{@headers.inspect}>"
113:     end
114: 
115:     private
116: 
117:     def normalize_header(key)
118:       key.to_s.downcase
119:     end
120:   end
121: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "net/http"
4: 
5: class LLM::Transport
6:   ##
7:   # The {LLM::Transport::HTTP LLM::Transport::HTTP} transport is the
8:   # built-in adapter for Ruby's {Net::HTTP Net::HTTP}. It manages
9:   # transient HTTP connections, tracks active requests by owner, and
10:   # interrupts in-flight requests when needed.
11:   #
12:   # @api private
13:   class HTTP < self
14:     include NetHTTPAdapter
15: 
16:     INTERRUPT_ERRORS = [::IOError, ::EOFError, Errno::EBADF].freeze
17:     ActiveRequest = Struct.new(:client, keyword_init: true)
18: 
19:     ##
20:     # @param [String] host
21:     # @param [Integer] port
22:     # @param [Integer] timeout
23:     # @param [Boolean] ssl
24:     # @return [LLM::Transport::HTTP]
25:     def initialize(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:)
26:       @host = host
27:       @port = port
28:       @timeout = timeout
29:       @ssl = ssl
30:       @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
31:       @monitor = Monitor.new
32:     end
33: 
34:     ##
35:     # Returns the current request owner.
36:     # @return [Object]
37:     def request_owner
38:       return Fiber.current unless defined?(::Async)
39:       Async::Task.current? ? Async::Task.current : Fiber.current
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # @return [Array<Class<Exception>>]
44:     def interrupt_errors
45:       [*INTERRUPT_ERRORS, *optional_interrupt_errors]
46:     end
47: 
48:     ##
49:     # Interrupt an active request, if any.
50:     # @param [Fiber] owner
51:     # @return [nil]
52:     def interrupt!(owner)
53:       req = request_for(owner) or return
54:       lock { (@interrupts ||= {})[owner] = true }
55:       close_socket(req.client)
56:       req.client.finish if req.client.active?
57:       owner.stop if owner.respond_to?(:stop)
58:     rescue *interrupt_errors
59:       nil
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Returns whether an execution owner was interrupted.
64:     # @param [Fiber] owner
65:     # @return [Boolean, nil]
66:     def interrupted?(owner)
67:       lock { @interrupts&.delete(owner) }
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Performs a request on the current HTTP transport.
72:     # Accepts both {Net::HTTPRequest} and {LLM::Transport::Request}.
73:     #
74:     # @param [Net::HTTPRequest, LLM::Transport::Request] request
75:     # @param [Fiber] owner
76:     # @param [LLM::Object, nil] stream
77:     # @yieldparam [LLM::Transport::Response] response
78:     # @return [Object]
79:     def request(request, owner:, stream: nil, &b)
80:       http_req = resolve_request(request)
81:       client = client()
82:       set_request(ActiveRequest.new(client:), owner)
83:       perform_request(client, http_req, stream, &b)
84:     ensure
85:       clear_request(owner)
86:     end
87: 
88:     ##
89:     # @return [String]
90:     def inspect
91:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)}>"
92:     end
93: 
94:     private
95: 
96:     attr_reader :host, :port, :timeout, :ssl, :base_uri
97: 
98:     def client
99:       client = Net::HTTP.new(host, port)
100:       client.read_timeout = timeout
101:       client.use_ssl = ssl
102:       client
103:     end
104: 
105:     def close_socket(http)
106:       socket = http&.instance_variable_get(:@socket) or return
107:       socket = socket.io if socket.respond_to?(:io)
108:       socket.close
109:     rescue *interrupt_errors
110:       nil
111:     end
112: 
113:     def request_for(owner)
114:       lock do
115:         @requests ||= {}
116:         @requests[owner]
117:       end
118:     end
119: 
120:     def set_request(req, owner)
121:       lock do
122:         @requests ||= {}
123:         @requests[owner] = req
124:       end
125:     end
126: 
127:     def clear_request(owner)
128:       lock { @requests&.delete(owner) }
129:     end
130: 
131:     def lock(&)
132:       @monitor.synchronize(&)
133:     end
134: 
135:     def optional_interrupt_errors
136:       defined?(::Async::Stop) ? [Async::Stop] : []
137:     end
138:   end
139: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # @api private
6:   module NetHTTPAdapter
7:     private
8: 
9:     def resolve_request(request)
10:       return request if ::Net::HTTPRequest === request
11:       build_net_http_request(request)
12:     end
13: 
14:     def build_net_http_request(req)
15:       method = req.method.downcase.to_sym
16:       path = req.path
17:       headers = req.headers
18:       http_req = case method
19:       when :get then ::Net::HTTP::Get.new(path, headers)
20:       when :post then ::Net::HTTP::Post.new(path, headers)
21:       when :put then ::Net::HTTP::Put.new(path, headers)
22:       when :patch then ::Net::HTTP::Patch.new(path, headers)
23:       when :delete then ::Net::HTTP::Delete.new(path, headers)
24:       else ::Net::HTTP::GenericRequest.new(method, path, nil, headers)
25:       end
26:       if req.body
27:         http_req.body = req.body
28:       elsif req.body_stream
29:         http_req.body_stream = req.body_stream
30:       end
31:       http_req
32:     end
33: 
34:     def perform_request(client, request, stream, &b)
35:       if stream
36:         client.request(request) do |raw|
37:           res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(raw)
38:           if res.success?
39:             parser = stream.decoder.new(stream.parser.new(stream.streamer))
40:             res.read_body(parser)
41:             body = parser.body
42:             res.body = (Hash === body || Array === body) ? LLM::Object.from(body) : body
43:           else
44:             body = +""
45:             res.read_body { body << _1 }
46:             res.body = body
47:           end
48:         ensure
49:           parser&.free
50:         end
51:       elsif b
52:         client.request(request) do |raw|
53:           res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(raw)
54:           res.success? ? b.call(res) : res
55:         end
56:       else
57:         LLM::Transport::Response.from(client.request(request))
58:       end
59:     end
60:   end
61: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder}
6:   # incrementally decodes streamed HTTP response bodies into parser
7:   # events.
8:   #
9:   # @api private
10:   class StreamDecoder
11:     ##
12:     # @return [Object]
13:     attr_reader :parser
14: 
15:     ##
16:     # @param [#parse!, #body, nil] parser
17:     # @yieldparam [Hash] chunk
18:     # @return [LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder]
19:     def initialize(parser = nil, &on_chunk)
20:       @buffer = +""
21:       @cursor = 0
22:       @data = []
23:       @parser = parser
24:       @on_chunk = on_chunk
25:     end
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [String] chunk
29:     # @return [void]
30:     def <<(chunk)
31:       @buffer << chunk
32:       each_line { handle_line(_1) }
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Object]
37:     def body
38:       parser.body
39:     end
40: 
41:     ##
42:     # @return [void]
43:     def free
44:       @buffer.clear
45:       @cursor = 0
46:       @data.clear
47:       parser.free if parser.respond_to?(:free)
48:     end
49: 
50:     private
51: 
52:     def handle_line(line)
53:       if line == "\n" || line == "\r\n"
54:         flush_sse_event
55:       elsif line.start_with?("data:")
56:         @data << field_value(line)
57:       elsif line.start_with?("event:", "id:", "retry:", ":")
58:       else
59:         decode!(strip_newline(line))
60:       end
61:     end
62: 
63:     def flush_sse_event
64:       return if @data.empty?
65:       decode!(@data.join("\n"))
66:       @data.clear
67:     end
68: 
69:     def field_value(line)
70:       value_start = line.getbyte(5) == 32 ? 6 : 5
71:       strip_newline(line.byteslice(value_start..))
72:     end
73: 
74:     def strip_newline(line)
75:       line = line.byteslice(0, line.bytesize - 1) if line.end_with?("\n")
76:       line = line.byteslice(0, line.bytesize - 1) if line.end_with?("\r")
77:       line
78:     end
79: 
80:     def decode!(payload)
81:       return if payload.empty? || payload == "[DONE]"
82:       chunk = LLM.json.load(payload)
83:       return unless chunk
84:       parser ? parser.parse!(chunk) : @on_chunk&.call(chunk)
85:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
86:     end
87: 
88:     def each_line
89:       while (newline = @buffer.index("\n", @cursor))
90:         line = @buffer[@cursor..newline]
91:         @cursor = newline + 1
92:         yield(line)
93:       end
94:       return if @cursor.zero?
95:       @buffer = @buffer[@cursor..] || +""
96:       @cursor = 0
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The superclass of all LLM errors
6:   class Error < RuntimeError
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response, nil]
9:     #  Returns the response associated with an error, or nil
10:     attr_accessor :response
11: 
12:     def initialize(...)
13:       block_given? ? yield(self) : nil
14:       super
15:     end
16: 
17:     def message
18:       if response
19:         [super, response.body].join("\n")
20:       else
21:         super
22:       end
23:     end
24:   end
25: 
26:   ##
27:   # HTTPUnauthorized
28:   UnauthorizedError = Class.new(Error)
29: 
30:   ##
31:   # HTTPTooManyRequests
32:   RateLimitError = Class.new(Error)
33: 
34:   ##
35:   # HTTPServerError
36:   ServerError = Class.new(Error)
37: 
38:   ##
39:   # HTTPNotFound
40:   NotFoundError = Class.new(Error)
41: 
42:   ##
43:   # When an given an input object that is not understood
44:   FormatError = Class.new(Error)
45: 
46:   ##
47:   # When given a prompt object that is not understood
48:   PromptError = Class.new(FormatError)
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # When given an invalid request
52:   InvalidRequestError = Class.new(Error)
53: 
54:   ##
55:   # When the context window is exceeded
56:   ContextWindowError = Class.new(InvalidRequestError)
57: 
58:   ##
59:   # When stuck in a tool call loop
60:   ToolLoopError = Class.new(Error)
61: 
62:   ##
63:   # When a guard blocks pending tool execution
64:   GuardError = Class.new(Error)
65: 
66:   ##
67:   # When a request is interrupted
68:   Interrupt = Class.new(Error)
69: 
70:   ##
71:   # When a concurrency strategy cannot execute a given tool
72:   RactorError = Class.new(Error)
73: 
74:   ##
75:   # When a tool call cannot be mapped to a local tool
76:   NoSuchToolError = Class.new(Error)
77: 
78:   ##
79:   # When {LLM::Registry} can't map a model
80:   NoSuchModelError = Class.new(Error)
81: 
82:   ##
83:   # When {LLM::Registry} can't map a registry
84:   NoSuchRegistryError = Class.new(Error)
85: 
86:   ##
87:   # When an optional runtime dependency cannot be required
88:   LoadError = Class.new(Error)
89: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The OpenAI class implements a provider for
6:   # [OpenAI](https://platform.openai.com/).
7:   #
8:   # @example
9:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
10:   #   require "llm"
11:   #
12:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
13:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
14:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
15:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
16:   class OpenAI < Provider
17:     require_relative "openai/error_handler"
18:     require_relative "openai/request_adapter"
19:     require_relative "openai/response_adapter"
20:     require_relative "openai/stream_parser"
21:     require_relative "openai/models"
22:     require_relative "openai/responses"
23:     require_relative "openai/images"
24:     require_relative "openai/audio"
25:     require_relative "openai/files"
26:     require_relative "openai/moderations"
27:     require_relative "openai/vector_stores"
28: 
29:     include RequestAdapter
30: 
31:     HOST = "api.openai.com"
32: 
33:     ##
34:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
35:     def initialize(base_path: "/v1", **)
36:       super(host: HOST, base_path:, **)
37:     end
38: 
39:     ##
40:     # @return [Symbol]
41:     #  Returns the provider's name
42:     def name
43:       :openai
44:     end
45: 
46:     ##
47:     # Provides an embedding
48:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create OpenAI docs
49:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
50:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
51:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
52:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
53:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#embed)
54:     def embed(input, model: "text-embedding-3-small", **params)
55:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path("/embeddings"), headers)
56:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:, model:}.merge!(params))
57:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
58:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
59:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
60:       res
61:     end
62: 
63:     ##
64:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
65:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create OpenAI docs
66:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
67:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
68:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
69:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
70:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
71:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
72:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
73:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
74:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
75:       req, messages = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
76:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
77:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
78:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
79:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
80:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
81:       res
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Provides an interface to OpenAI's response API
86:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/responses/create OpenAI docs
87:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Responses]
88:     def responses
89:       LLM::OpenAI::Responses.new(self)
90:     end
91: 
92:     ##
93:     # Provides an interface to OpenAI's image generation API
94:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/images/create OpenAI docs
95:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Images]
96:     def images
97:       LLM::OpenAI::Images.new(self)
98:     end
99: 
100:     ##
101:     # Provides an interface to OpenAI's audio generation API
102:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/audio/createSpeech OpenAI docs
103:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Audio]
104:     def audio
105:       LLM::OpenAI::Audio.new(self)
106:     end
107: 
108:     ##
109:     # Provides an interface to OpenAI's files API
110:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/create OpenAI docs
111:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Files]
112:     def files
113:       LLM::OpenAI::Files.new(self)
114:     end
115: 
116:     ##
117:     # Provides an interface to OpenAI's models API
118:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/models/list OpenAI docs
119:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Models]
120:     def models
121:       LLM::OpenAI::Models.new(self)
122:     end
123: 
124:     ##
125:     # Provides an interface to OpenAI's moderation API
126:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/moderations/create OpenAI docs
127:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/models#moderation OpenAI moderation models
128:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Moderations]
129:     def moderations
130:       LLM::OpenAI::Moderations.new(self)
131:     end
132: 
133:     ##
134:     # Provides an interface to OpenAI's vector store API
135:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/vector-stores/create OpenAI docs
136:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::VectorStore]
137:     def vector_stores
138:       LLM::OpenAI::VectorStores.new(self)
139:     end
140: 
141:     ##
142:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
143:     def assistant_role
144:       "assistant"
145:     end
146: 
147:     ##
148:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
149:     # @see https://platform.openai.com/docs/models/gpt-4.1 gpt-4.1
150:     # @return [String]
151:     def default_model
152:       "gpt-4.1"
153:     end
154: 
155:     ##
156:     # @note
157:     #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
158:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
159:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
160:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
161:     def server_tools
162:       {
163:         web_search: server_tool(:web_search),
164:         file_search: server_tool(:file_search),
165:         image_generation: server_tool(:image_generation),
166:         code_interpreter: server_tool(:code_interpreter),
167:         computer_use: server_tool(:computer_use)
168:       }
169:     end
170: 
171:     ##
172:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
173:     # OpenAI web search tool.
174:     # @example
175:     #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
176:     #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
177:     #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
178:     # @param query [String] The search query.
179:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
180:     def web_search(query:)
181:       ResponseAdapter.adapt(
182:         responses.create(query, store: false, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]),
183:         type: :web_search
184:       )
185:     end
186: 
187:     private
188: 
189:     def completions_path
190:       path("/chat/completions")
191:     end
192: 
193:     def headers
194:       lock do
195:         (@headers || {}).merge(
196:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
197:           "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
198:         )
199:       end
200:     end
201: 
202:     def stream_parser
203:       LLM::OpenAI::StreamParser
204:     end
205: 
206:     def error_handler
207:       LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler
208:     end
209: 
210:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
211:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
212:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
213:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
214:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
215:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
216:       if params[:stream]
217:         params[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}.merge!(params[:stream_options] || {})
218:       end
219:       [params, stream, tools, role]
220:     end
221: 
222:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
223:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
224:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: adapt(messages, mode: :complete).flatten}.merge!(params))
225:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(completions_path, headers)
226:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
227:       [req, messages]
228:     end
229: 
230:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
231:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
232:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
233:       else
234:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
235:       end
236:     end
237: 
238:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
239:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
240:       value = message&.content || fallback
241:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
242:     end
243:   end
244: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8:     require_relative "request_adapter/respond"
9:     require_relative "request_adapter/moderation"
10: 
11:     ##
12:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
13:     #  The messages to adapt
14:     # @param [Symbol] mode
15:     #  The mode to adapt the messages for
16:     # @return [Array<Hash>]
17:     def adapt(messages, mode: :complete)
18:       messages.filter_map do |message|
19:         if mode == :complete
20:           Completion.new(message).adapt
21:         else
22:           Respond.new(message).adapt
23:         end
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     private
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param [Hash] params
31:     # @return [Hash]
32:     def adapt_schema(params)
33:       return {} unless params and params[:schema]
34:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
35:       schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
36:       {
37:         response_format: {
38:           type: "json_schema",
39:           json_schema: {name: "JSONSchema", schema:}
40:         }
41:       }
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # @param [Hash] params
46:     # @return [Hash]
47:     def adapt_tools(tools)
48:       if tools.nil? || tools.empty?
49:         {}
50:       else
51:         {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
52:       end
53:     end
54:   end
55: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::OpenAI::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the OpenAI chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         elsif message.tool_call?
22:           {role: message.role, content: nil, tool_calls: message.extra[:original_tool_calls]}
23:         else
24:           adapt_message
25:         end
26:       end
27:     end
28: 
29:     private
30: 
31:     def adapt_message
32:       case content
33:       when Array
34:         adapt_array
35:       else
36:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def adapt_array
41:       if content.empty?
42:         nil
43:       elsif returns.any?
44:         returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
45:       else
46:         {role: message.role, content: content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }}
47:       end
48:     end
49: 
50:     def adapt_content(content)
51:       case content
52:       when LLM::Object
53:         adapt_object(content)
54:       when String
55:         [{type: :text, text: content.to_s}]
56:       when LLM::Response
57:         adapt_remote_file(content)
58:       when LLM::Message
59:         adapt_content(content.content)
60:       when LLM::Function::Return
61:         throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
62:       else
63:         prompt_error!(content)
64:       end
65:     end
66: 
67:     def adapt_object(object)
68:       case object.kind
69:       when :image_url
70:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: object.value}}]
71:       when :local_file
72:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
73:       when :remote_file
74:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
75:       else
76:         prompt_error!(object)
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def adapt_local_file(file)
81:       if file.image?
82:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: file.to_data_uri}}]
83:       else
84:         [{type: :file, file: {filename: file.basename, file_data: file.to_data_uri}}]
85:       end
86:     end
87: 
88:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
89:       if file.file?
90:         [{type: :file, file: {file_id: file.id}}]
91:       else
92:         prompt_error!(file)
93:       end
94:     end
95: 
96:     def prompt_error!(content)
97:       if LLM::Object === content
98:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
99:                                 "supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
100:       else
101:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
102:                                 "is not supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def message = @message
107:     def content = message.content
108:     def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
109:   end
110: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module ResponseAdapter
7:     require_relative "response_adapter/audio"
8:     require_relative "response_adapter/completion"
9:     require_relative "response_adapter/embedding"
10:     require_relative "response_adapter/enumerable"
11:     require_relative "response_adapter/file"
12:     require_relative "response_adapter/image"
13:     require_relative "response_adapter/moderations"
14:     require_relative "response_adapter/models"
15:     require_relative "response_adapter/responds"
16:     require_relative "response_adapter/web_search"
17: 
18:     module_function
19: 
20:     ##
21:     # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
22:     # @param [Symbol] type
23:     # @return [LLM::Response]
24:     def adapt(res, type:)
25:       response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
26:       adapter = select(type)
27:       response.extend(adapter)
28:     end
29: 
30:     ##
31:     # @api private
32:     def select(type)
33:       case type
34:       when :audio then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Audio
35:       when :completion then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Completion
36:       when :embedding then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Embedding
37:       when :enumerable then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Enumerable
38:       when :file then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::File
39:       when :image then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Image
40:       when :moderations then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Moderations
41:       when :models then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Models
42:       when :responds then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Responds
43:       when :web_search then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::WebSearch
44:       else
45:         raise ArgumentError, "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
46:       end
47:     end
48:   end
49: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter
4:   module Completion
5:     ##
6:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#messages)
7:     def messages
8:       body.choices.map.with_index do |choice, index|
9:         message = choice.message
10:         extra = {
11:           index:, response: self,
12:           logprobs: choice.logprobs,
13:           reasoning_content: message.reasoning_content,
14:           tool_calls: adapt_tool_calls(message.tool_calls),
15:           original_tool_calls: message.tool_calls
16:         }
17:         LLM::Message.new(message.role, message.content, extra)
18:       end
19:     end
20:     alias_method :choices, :messages
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_tokens)
24:     def input_tokens
25:       body.usage&.prompt_tokens || 0
26:     end
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_tokens)
30:     def output_tokens
31:       body.usage&.completion_tokens || 0
32:     end
33: 
34:     ##
35:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_tokens)
36:     def reasoning_tokens
37:       body
38:         .usage
39:         &.completion_tokens_details
40:         &.reasoning_tokens || 0
41:     end
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_audio_tokens)
45:     def input_audio_tokens
46:       body
47:         .usage
48:         &.prompt_tokens_details
49:         &.audio_tokens || 0
50:     end
51: 
52:     ##
53:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_audio_tokens)
54:     def output_audio_tokens
55:       body
56:         .usage
57:         &.completion_tokens_details
58:         &.audio_tokens || 0
59:     end
60: 
61:     ##
62:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_image_tokens)
63:     def input_image_tokens
64:       body
65:         .usage
66:         &.prompt_tokens_details
67:         &.image_tokens || 0
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_read_tokens)
72:     def cache_read_tokens
73:       body
74:         .usage
75:         &.prompt_tokens_details
76:         &.cached_tokens || 0
77:     end
78: 
79:     ##
80:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_write_tokens)
81:     def cache_write_tokens
82:       0
83:     end
84: 
85:     ##
86:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#total_tokens)
87:     def total_tokens
88:       body.usage&.total_tokens || 0
89:     end
90: 
91:     ##
92:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#usage)
93:     def usage
94:       super
95:     end
96: 
97:     ##
98:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#model)
99:     def model
100:       body.model
101:     end
102: 
103:     ##
104:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content)
105:     def content
106:       super
107:     end
108: 
109:     ##
110:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_content)
111:     def reasoning_content
112:       super
113:     end
114: 
115:     ##
116:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content!)
117:     def content!
118:       super
119:     end
120: 
121:     private
122: 
123:     def adapt_tool_calls(tools)
124:       (tools || []).filter_map do |tool|
125:         next unless tool.function
126:         {id: tool.id, name: tool.function.name, arguments: parse_tool_arguments(tool.function.arguments)}
127:       end
128:     end
129: 
130:     def parse_tool_arguments(arguments)
131:       return {} if arguments.to_s.empty?
132:       parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
133:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
134:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
135:       {}
136:     end
137: 
138:     include LLM::Contract::Completion
139:   end
140: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.unauthorized?
55:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
56:       elsif res.rate_limited?
57:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
58:       elsif res.not_found?
59:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
60:       else
61:         error = body["error"] || {}
62:         case error["type"]
63:         when "invalid_request_error" then handle_invalid_request(error)
64:         when "server_error"
65:           LLM::ServerError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
66:         else
67:           LLM::Error.new(error["message"] || "Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @param [Exception] error
74:     # @return [LLM::Error]
75:     def handle_invalid_request(error)
76:       case error["code"]
77:       when "context_length_exceeded"
78:         LLM::ContextWindowError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
79:       else
80:         LLM::InvalidRequestError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
86:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
87:     def parse_body!
88:       if String === res.body
89:         LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
90:       elsif Hash === res.body
91:         LLM::Object.from(res.body)
92:       else
93:         res.body
94:       end
95:     rescue
96:       res.body
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

(End of file - total 99 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     EMPTY_HASH = {}.freeze
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
11:     # @return [Hash]
12:     attr_reader :body
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: {}}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
22:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
23:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
24:     end
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [Hash] chunk
28:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
29:     def parse!(chunk)
30:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
31:     end
32: 
33:     ##
34:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
35:     # @return [void]
36:     def free
37:       @emits.clear
38:     end
39: 
40:     private
41: 
42:     def merge!(chunk)
43:       chunk.each do |key, value|
44:         if key == "choices"
45:           @body["choices"] ||= []
46:           merge_choices!(value)
47:         else
48:           @body[key] = value
49:         end
50:       end
51:     end
52: 
53:     def merge_choices!(choices)
54:       body_choices = @body["choices"]
55:       choices.each do |choice|
56:         index = choice["index"]
57:         delta = choice["delta"] || EMPTY_HASH
58:         target_message = if body_choice = body_choices[index]
59:           body_choice["message"]
60:         else
61:           body_choices[index] = {"message" => {"role" => "assistant"}}
62:           body_choices[index]["message"]
63:         end
64:         merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
65:       end
66:     end
67: 
68:     def merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
69:       if delta.length == 1
70:         merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
71:       elsif content = delta["content"]
72:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
73:           target_content << content
74:         else
75:           target_message["content"] = content
76:         end
77:         emit_content(content)
78:       elsif reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
79:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
80:           target_reasoning << reasoning
81:         else
82:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
83:         end
84:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
85:       elsif tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
86:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
87:       end
88:       return if delta.length <= 1
89:       delta.each do |key, value|
90:         next if value.nil? || key == "content" || key == "reasoning_content" || key == "tool_calls"
91:         target_message[key] = value
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
96:       if content = delta["content"]
97:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
98:           target_content << content
99:         else
100:           target_message["content"] = content
101:         end
102:         emit_content(content)
103:         return
104:       end
105:       if reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
106:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
107:           target_reasoning << reasoning
108:         else
109:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
110:         end
111:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
112:         return
113:       end
114:       if tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
115:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     def merge_tools!(target, tools)
120:       target["tool_calls"] ||= []
121:       tools.each.with_index do |toola, index|
122:         tindex = toola["index"]
123:         tindex = index unless Integer === tindex && tindex >= 0
124:         toolb = target["tool_calls"][tindex]
125:         functiona = toola["function"]
126:         functionb = toolb && toolb["function"]
127:         if functiona && functionb
128:           # Append to existing function arguments
129:           merge_function!(functionb, functiona)
130:         else
131:           target["tool_calls"][tindex] = toola
132:         end
133:         emit_tool(target["tool_calls"][tindex], tindex)
134:       end
135:     end
136: 
137:     def merge_function!(target, source)
138:       if arguments = source["arguments"]
139:         if target_arguments = target["arguments"]
140:           target_arguments << arguments
141:         else
142:           target["arguments"] = arguments
143:         end
144:       end
145:       if name = source["name"]
146:         if target_name = target["name"]
147:           target_name << name
148:         else
149:           target["name"] = name
150:         end
151:       end
152:       return if source.length <= 2
153:       source.each do |func_key, func_value|
154:         next if func_key == "arguments" || func_key == "name"
155:         target[func_key] ||= +""
156:         target[func_key] << func_value
157:       end
158:     end
159: 
160:     def emit_content(value)
161:       if @can_emit_content
162:         @stream.on_content(value)
163:       elsif @can_push_content
164:         @stream << value
165:       end
166:     end
167: 
168:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
169:       if @can_emit_reasoning_content
170:         @stream.on_reasoning_content(value)
171:       end
172:     end
173: 
174:     def emit_tool(tool, tindex)
175:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
176:       return if @emits[:tools][tindex]
177:       function = tool["function"]
178:       return unless function && tool["id"] && function["name"]
179:       return unless arguments_complete?(function["arguments"])
180:       arguments = parse_arguments(function["arguments"])
181:       return unless arguments
182:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
183:       @emits[:tools][tindex] = true
184:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
188:       registered = @stream.__find__(function["name"])
189:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(function["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
190:         fn.id = tool["id"]
191:         fn.arguments = arguments
192:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
193:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
194:       end
195:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
196:     end
197: 
198:     def parse_arguments(arguments)
199:       return nil if arguments.to_s.empty?
200:       parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
201:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : nil
202:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
203:       nil
204:     end
205: 
206:     def arguments_complete?(arguments)
207:       value = arguments.to_s.rstrip
208:       !value.empty? && value.end_with?("}")
209:     end
210:   end
211: end

(End of file - total 211 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Anthropic class implements a provider for
6:   # [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com).
7:   #
8:   # @example
9:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
10:   #   require "llm"
11:   #
12:   #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
13:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
14:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
15:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
16:   class Anthropic < Provider
17:     require_relative "anthropic/utils"
18:     require_relative "anthropic/error_handler"
19:     require_relative "anthropic/request_adapter"
20:     require_relative "anthropic/response_adapter"
21:     require_relative "anthropic/stream_parser"
22:     require_relative "anthropic/models"
23:     require_relative "anthropic/files"
24:     include RequestAdapter
25:     extend Utils
26: 
27:     HOST = "api.anthropic.com"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :anthropic
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
44:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/messages Anthropic docs
45:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
47:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
48:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
49:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
50:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
51:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
52:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
53:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
54:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
55:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
56:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
57:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
58:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
59:       res
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's models API
64:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/models-list
65:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Models]
66:     def models
67:       LLM::Anthropic::Models.new(self)
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's files API
72:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/files Anthropic docs
73:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Files]
74:     def files
75:       LLM::Anthropic::Files.new(self)
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
80:     def assistant_role
81:       "assistant"
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Anthropic expects tool results to be sent as user messages
86:     # containing `tool_result` content blocks rather than a distinct
87:     # `tool` role.
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
89:     def tool_role
90:       :user
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/all-models#model-comparison-table claude-sonnet-4-20250514
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # @note
103:     #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
104:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
105:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
106:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/tool-use/web-search-tool Anthropic docs
107:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
108:     def server_tools
109:       {
110:         bash: server_tool(:bash, type: "bash_20250124"),
111:         web_search: server_tool(:web_search, type: "web_search_20250305", max_uses: 5),
112:         text_editor: server_tool(:str_replace_based_edit_tool, type: "text_editor_20250728", max_characters: 10_000)
113:       }
114:     end
115: 
116:     ##
117:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
118:     # Anthropic web search tool.
119:     # @example
120:     #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
121:     #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
122:     #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
123:     # @param query [String] The search query.
124:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
125:     def web_search(query:)
126:       ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]), type: :web_search)
127:     end
128: 
129:     private
130: 
131:     def headers
132:       lock do
133:         (@headers || {}).merge(
134:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
135:           "x-api-key" => @key,
136:           "anthropic-version" => "2023-06-01",
137:           "anthropic-beta" => "files-api-2025-04-14"
138:         )
139:       end
140:     end
141: 
142:     def stream_parser
143:       LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser
144:     end
145: 
146:     def error_handler
147:       LLM::Anthropic::ErrorHandler
148:     end
149: 
150:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
151:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 1024}.merge!(params)
152:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
153:       params = [params, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
154:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
155:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
156:       [params, stream, tools, role]
157:     end
158: 
159:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
160:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
161:       payload = adapt(messages)
162:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload.merge!(params))
163:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/messages", headers)
164:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
165:       req
166:     end
167: 
168:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
169:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
170:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
171:       else
172:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
173:       end
174:     end
175:   end
176: end
