1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Google class implements a provider for
6:   # [Gemini](https://ai.google.dev/). The Google provider
7:   # can accept multiple inputs (text, images, audio, and video).
8:   # The inputs can be provided inline via the prompt for files
9:   # under 20MB or via the Gemini Files API for files
10:   # that are over 20MB.
11:   #
12:   # @example
13:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
14:   #   require "llm"
15:   #
16:   #   llm = LLM.google(key: ENV["KEY"])
17:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
18:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
19:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
20:   class Google < Provider
21:     require_relative "google/utils"
22:     require_relative "google/error_handler"
23:     require_relative "google/request_adapter"
24:     require_relative "google/response_adapter"
25:     require_relative "google/stream_parser"
26:     require_relative "google/models"
27:     require_relative "google/images"
28:     require_relative "google/audio"
29:     require_relative "google/files"
30: 
31:     include RequestAdapter
32:     extend Utils
33: 
34:     HOST = "generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
35: 
36:     ##
37:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
38:     def initialize(**)
39:       super(host: HOST, **)
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # @return [Symbol]
44:     #  Returns the provider's name
45:     def name
46:       :google
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # Provides an embedding
51:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
52:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
53:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
54:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
55:     # @return [LLM::Response]
56:     def embed(input, model: "gemini-embedding-001", **params)
57:       model = model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
58:       path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", "embedContent?key=#{@key}"].join(":")
59:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
60:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({content: {parts: [{text: input}]}})
61:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
62:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
63:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
64:       res
65:     end
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
69:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content#v1beta.models.generateContent Gemini docs
70:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
71:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
72:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
73:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
74:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
75:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
76:     # @return [LLM::Response]
77:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
78:       params, stream, tools, role, model = normalize_complete_params(params)
79:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
80:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model:)
81:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
82:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
83:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model:, res:, span:)
84:       res
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's audio API
89:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/audio Gemini docs
90:     # @return [LLM::Google::Audio]
91:     def audio
92:       LLM::Google::Audio.new(self)
93:     end
94: 
95:     ##
96:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's image generation API
97:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-generation Gemini docs
98:     # @return [see LLM::Google::Images]
99:     def images
100:       LLM::Google::Images.new(self)
101:     end
102: 
103:     ##
104:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's file management API
105:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/files Gemini docs
106:     # @return [LLM::Google::Files]
107:     def files
108:       LLM::Google::Files.new(self)
109:     end
110: 
111:     ##
112:     # Provides an interface to Gemini's models API
113:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models Gemini docs
114:     # @return [LLM::Google::Models]
115:     def models
116:       LLM::Google::Models.new(self)
117:     end
118: 
119:     ##
120:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
121:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models#gemini-2.5-flash gemini-2.5-flash
122:     # @return [String]
123:     def default_model
124:       "gemini-2.5-flash"
125:     end
126: 
127:     ##
128:     # @note
129:     #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
130:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
131:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
132:     # @see https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/google-search Gemini docs
133:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
134:     def server_tools
135:       {
136:         google_search: server_tool(:google_search),
137:         code_execution: server_tool(:code_execution),
138:         url_context: server_tool(:url_context)
139:       }
140:     end
141: 
142:     ##
143:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
144:     # Google Search tool.
145:     # @param query [String] The search query.
146:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
147:     def web_search(query:)
148:       ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:google_search]]), type: :web_search)
149:     end
150: 
151:     ##
152:     # @return [Symbol]
153:     #  Returns the providers user role
154:     def user_role
155:       :user
156:     end
157: 
158:     ##
159:     # @return [Symbol]
160:     #  Returns the providers system role
161:     def system_role
162:       :user
163:     end
164: 
165:     ##
166:     # @return [Symbol]
167:     # Returns the providers developer role
168:     def developer_role
169:       :user
170:     end
171: 
172:     ##
173:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
174:     def assistant_role
175:       "model"
176:     end
177: 
178:     private
179: 
180:     def headers
181:       lock do
182:         (@headers || {}).merge(
183:           "Content-Type" => "application/json"
184:         )
185:       end
186:     end
187: 
188:     def stream_parser
189:       LLM::Google::StreamParser
190:     end
191: 
192:     def error_handler
193:       LLM::Google::ErrorHandler
194:     end
195: 
196:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
197:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model}.merge!(params)
198:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
199:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
200:       role, model, stream = [:role, :model, :stream].map { params.delete(_1) }
201:       [params, stream, tools, role, model]
202:     end
203: 
204:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, model, stream)
205:       action = stream ? "streamGenerateContent?key=#{@key}&alt=sse" : "generateContent?key=#{@key}"
206:       model.respond_to?(:id) ? model.id : model
207:       path = ["/v1beta/models/#{model}", action].join(":")
208:       req  = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
209:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
210:       body = LLM.json.dump({contents: adapt(messages)}.merge!(params))
211:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
212:       req
213:     end
214: 
215:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
216:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
217:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
218:       else
219:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
220:       end
221:     end
222:   end
223: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Ollama class implements a provider for [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) –
6:   # and the provider supports a wide range of models. It is straight forward
7:   # to run on your own hardware, and there are a number of multi-modal models
8:   # that can process both images and text.
9:   #
10:   # @example
11:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
12:   #   require "llm"
13:   #
14:   #   llm = LLM.ollama(key: nil)
15:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, model: "llava")
16:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this image", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
17:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
18:   class Ollama < Provider
19:     require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
20:     require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
21:     require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
22:     require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
23:     require_relative "ollama/models"
24: 
25:     include RequestAdapter
26: 
27:     HOST = "localhost"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :ollama
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an embedding
44:     # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
45:     # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
47:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
48:     # @return [LLM::Response]
49:     def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
50:       params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
51:       req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
52:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
53:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
54:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
55:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
56:       res
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
61:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-a-chat-completion Ollama docs
62:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
63:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
64:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
65:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
66:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
67:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
68:     # @return [LLM::Response]
69:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
70:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
71:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
72:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
73:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
74:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
75:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
76:       res
77:     end
78: 
79:     ##
80:     # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
81:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
82:     # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
83:     def models
84:       LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
85:     end
86: 
87:     ##
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
89:     def assistant_role
90:       "assistant"
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://ollama.com/library/qwen3 qwen3
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "qwen3:latest"
99:     end
100: 
101:     private
102: 
103:     def headers
104:       lock do
105:         (@headers || {}).merge(
106:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
107:           "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
108:         )
109:       end
110:     end
111: 
112:     def stream_parser
113:       LLM::Ollama::StreamParser
114:     end
115: 
116:     def error_handler
117:       LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler
118:     end
119: 
120:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
121:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
122:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
123:       params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
124:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
125:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
126:       [params, stream, tools, role]
127:     end
128: 
129:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
130:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
131:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
132:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
133:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
134:       req
135:     end
136: 
137:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
138:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
139:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
140:       else
141:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
142:       end
143:     end
144:   end
145: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
6:   # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
7:   #
8:   # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
9:   # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
10:   # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
11:   # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
12:   #
13:   # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
14:   # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
15:   # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
16:   # instead of a single API key.
17:   #
18:   # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
19:   # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
20:   # Ruby's stdlib.
21:   #
22:   # @example
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
26:   #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
27:   #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
28:   #     region: "us-east-1"
29:   #   )
30:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
31:   #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
32:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }
33:   class Bedrock < Provider
34:     require_relative "bedrock/signature"
35:     require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"
36:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter"
37:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter/completion"
38:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter"
39:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/completion"
40:     require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/models"
41:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_decoder"
42:     require_relative "bedrock/stream_parser"
43:     require_relative "bedrock/models"
44: 
45:     include RequestAdapter
46: 
47:     HOST_PATTERN = "bedrock-runtime.%s.amazonaws.com"
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @param [String] access_key_id AWS access key ID
51:     # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key
52:     # @param [String] region AWS region (e.g. "us-east-1")
53:     # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
54:     # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
55:     # @param [Integer] port Connection port
56:     # @param [Boolean] ssl Whether to use SSL
57:     # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds
58:     def initialize(access_key_id: nil, secret_access_key: nil,
59:                    region: nil, session_token: nil,
60:                    host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,
61:                    **)
62:       region ||= "us-east-1"
63:       @access_key_id = access_key_id
64:       @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
65:       @aws_region = region
66:       @session_token = session_token
67:       host ||= HOST_PATTERN % region
68:       @aws_host = host
69:       super(key: @access_key_id, host:, port:, ssl:, timeout:, persistent: false)
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @return [Symbol] Returns the provider's name
74:     def name
75:       :bedrock
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # Provides an interface to the Bedrock Converse API
80:     #
81:     # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_runtime_Converse.html
82:     #
83:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
84:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
85:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
86:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
87:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
88:       req, messages, body = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream:)
89:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
90:       sign!(req, body)
91:       model_id = model_id_for(req.path)
92:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, operation: "chat", stream_parser:, model: model_id)
93:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
94:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
95:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: model_id, res:, span:)
96:       res
97:     end
98: 
99:     ##
100:     # Provides an interface to Bedrock's ListFoundationModels API.
101:     #
102:     # @note
103:     #  Unlike the Converse API (bedrock-runtime), this endpoint lives
104:     #  on the control plane (bedrock.<region>.amazonaws.com).
105:     #
106:     # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_ListFoundationModels.html
107:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::Models]
108:     def models
109:       LLM::Bedrock::Models.new(self)
110:     end
111: 
112:     ##
113:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
114:     def files
115:       raise NotImplementedError
116:     end
117: 
118:     ##
119:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
120:     def images
121:       raise NotImplementedError
122:     end
123: 
124:     ##
125:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
126:     def audio
127:       raise NotImplementedError
128:     end
129: 
130:     ##
131:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
132:     def moderations
133:       raise NotImplementedError
134:     end
135: 
136:     ##
137:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
138:     def responses
139:       raise NotImplementedError
140:     end
141: 
142:     ##
143:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
144:     def vector_stores
145:       raise NotImplementedError
146:     end
147: 
148:     ##
149:     # @raise [NotImplementedError]
150:     def embed(input, model: nil, **params)
151:       raise NotImplementedError
152:     end
153: 
154:     ##
155:     # @return [String]
156:     def assistant_role
157:       "assistant"
158:     end
159: 
160:     ##
161:     # Bedrock expects tool results as user messages containing
162:     # `toolResult` content blocks rather than a distinct `tool` role.
163:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
164:     def tool_role
165:       :user
166:     end
167: 
168:     ##
169:     # @return [String]
170:     def default_model
171:       "deepseek.v3.2"
172:     end
173: 
174:     private
175: 
176:     def headers
177:       lock do
178:         (@headers || {}).merge("Content-Type" => "application/json")
179:       end
180:     end
181: 
182:     def credentials
183:       LLM::Object.from(
184:         access_key_id: @access_key_id,
185:         secret_access_key: @secret_access_key,
186:         aws_region: @aws_region,
187:         host: @aws_host,
188:         session_token: @session_token
189:       )
190:     end
191: 
192:     def stream_parser
193:       LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser
194:     end
195: 
196:     def error_handler
197:       LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler
198:     end
199: 
200:     def stream_decoder
201:       LLM::Bedrock::StreamDecoder
202:     end
203: 
204:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
205:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 2048}.merge!(params)
206:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
207:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
208:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
209:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
210:       [params, stream, tools, role]
211:     end
212: 
213:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream: nil)
214:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
215:       model_id = params.delete(:model) || default_model
216:       payload = build_converse_payload(messages, params)
217:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload)
218:       path = stream ? "/model/#{model_id}/converse-stream" \
219:                     : "/model/#{model_id}/converse"
220:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
221:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
222:       [req, messages, body]
223:     end
224: 
225:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
226:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
227:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
228:       else
229:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
230:       end
231:     end
232: 
233:     def build_converse_payload(messages, params)
234:       adapted = adapt(messages)
235:       payload = {}
236:       payload[:system] = adapted[:system] if adapted[:system]&.any?
237:       payload[:messages] = adapted[:messages]
238:       inference_config = {}
239:       inference_config[:maxTokens] = params.delete(:max_tokens) if params[:max_tokens]
240:       inference_config[:temperature] = params.delete(:temperature) if params.key?(:temperature)
241:       inference_config[:topP] = params.delete(:top_p) if params.key?(:top_p)
242:       inference_config[:stopSequences] = params.delete(:stop) if params[:stop]
243:       payload[:inferenceConfig] = inference_config unless inference_config.empty?
244:       payload[:toolConfig] = params.delete(:toolConfig) if params[:toolConfig]
245:       payload[:outputConfig] = params.delete(:outputConfig) if params[:outputConfig]
246:       additional = {}
247:       top_k = params.delete(:top_k)
248:       additional[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
249:       payload[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional unless additional.empty?
250:       payload
251:     end
252: 
253:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
254:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
255:       value = message&.content || fallback
256:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
257:     end
258: 
259:     def model_id_for(path)
260:       path[%r{\A/model/(.+?)/converse(?:-stream)?\z}, 1] || default_model
261:     end
262: 
263:     def sign!(req, body)
264:       Signature.new(
265:         credentials:,
266:         method: req.method,
267:         path: req.path,
268:         body:
269:       ).sign!(req)
270:     end
271:   end
272: end

(End of file - total 272 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Context LLM::Context} is the stateful execution boundary in
6:   # llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # It holds the evolving runtime state for an LLM workflow:
9:   # conversation history, tool calls and returns, schema and streaming
10:   # configuration, accumulated usage, and request ownership for
11:   # interruption.
12:   #
13:   # This is broader than prompt context alone. A context is the object
14:   # that lets one-off prompts, streaming turns, tool execution,
15:   # persistence, retries, and serialized long-lived workflows all run
16:   # through the same model.
17:   #
18:   # A context can drive the chat completions API that all providers
19:   # support or the Responses API on providers that expose it.
20:   #
21:   # @example
22:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
26:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
27:   #
28:   #   prompt = LLM::Prompt.new(llm) do
29:   #     system "Be concise and show your reasoning briefly."
30:   #     user "If a train goes 60 mph for 1.5 hours, how far does it travel?"
31:   #     user "Now double the speed for the same time."
32:   #   end
33:   #
34:   #   ctx.talk(prompt)
35:   #   ctx.messages.each { |m| puts "[#{m.role}] #{m.content}" }
36:   class Context
37:     require_relative "compactor"
38:     require_relative "context/serializer"
39:     require_relative "context/deserializer"
40:     include Serializer
41:     include Deserializer
42: 
43:     ZERO_USAGE = LLM::Object.from(
44:       input_tokens: 0,
45:       output_tokens: 0,
46:       reasoning_tokens: 0,
47:       input_audio_tokens: 0,
48:       output_audio_tokens: 0,
49:       input_image_tokens: 0,
50:       cache_read_tokens: 0,
51:       cache_write_tokens: 0,
52:       total_tokens: 0
53:     )
54:     private_constant :ZERO_USAGE
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Returns the accumulated message history for this context
58:     # @return [LLM::Buffer<LLM::Message>]
59:     attr_reader :messages
60: 
61:     ##
62:     # Returns a provider
63:     # @return [LLM::Provider]
64:     attr_reader :llm
65: 
66:     ##
67:     # Returns the context mode
68:     # @return [Symbol]
69:     attr_reader :mode
70: 
71:     ##
72:     # @param [LLM::Provider] llm
73:     #  A provider
74:     # @param [Hash] params
75:     #  The parameters to maintain throughout the conversation.
76:     #  Any parameter the provider supports can be included and
77:     #  not only those listed here.
78:     # @option params [Symbol] :mode Defaults to :completions
79:     # @option params [String] :model Defaults to the provider's default model
80:     # @option params [Array<LLM::Function>, nil] :tools Defaults to nil
81:     # @option params [Array<String>, nil] :skills Defaults to nil
82:     def initialize(llm, params = {})
83:       @llm = llm
84:       @mode = params.delete(:mode) || :completions
85:       @compactor = params.delete(:compactor)
86:       @guard = params.delete(:guard)
87:       @transformer = params.delete(:transformer)
88:       tools = [*params.delete(:tools), *load_skills(params.delete(:skills))]
89:       @params = {model: llm.default_model, schema: nil}.compact.merge!(params)
90:       @params[:tools] = tools unless tools.empty?
91:       @messages = LLM::Buffer.new(llm)
92:     end
93: 
94:     ##
95:     # Returns the default params for this context
96:     # @return [Hash]
97:     def params
98:       @params.dup
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # Returns a context compactor
103:     # This feature is inspired by the compaction approach developed by
104:     # General Intelligence Systems.
105:     # @return [LLM::Compactor]
106:     def compactor
107:       @compactor = LLM::Compactor.new(self, @compactor || {}) unless LLM::Compactor === @compactor
108:       @compactor
109:     end
110: 
111:     ##
112:     # Sets a context compactor or compactor config
113:     # @param [LLM::Compactor, Hash, nil] compactor
114:     # @return [LLM::Compactor, Hash, nil]
115:     def compactor=(compactor)
116:       @compactor = compactor
117:     end
118: 
119:     ##
120:     # Returns whether the context has been compacted and no later model
121:     # response has cleared that state.
122:     # @return [Boolean]
123:     # @api private
124:     attr_accessor :compacted
125:     alias_method :compacted?, :compacted
126: 
127:     ##
128:     # Returns a guard, if configured.
129:     #
130:     # Guards are context-level supervisors for agentic execution. A guard can
131:     # inspect the runtime state and decide whether pending tool work should be
132:     # blocked before the context keeps looping.
133:     #
134:     # The built-in implementation is {LLM::LoopGuard LLM::LoopGuard}, which
135:     # detects repeated tool-call patterns and turns them into in-band
136:     # {LLM::GuardError LLM::GuardError} tool returns.
137:     #
138:     # @return [#call, nil]
139:     def guard
140:       return if @guard.nil? || @guard == false
141:       @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new if @guard == true
142:       @guard = LLM::LoopGuard.new(@guard) if Hash === @guard
143:       @guard
144:     end
145: 
146:     ##
147:     # Sets a guard or guard config.
148:     #
149:     # Guards must implement `call(ctx)` and return either `nil` or a warning
150:     # string. Returning a warning tells the context to block pending tool work
151:     # with guarded tool errors instead of continuing the loop.
152:     #
153:     # @param [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil] guard
154:     # @return [#call, Hash, Boolean, nil]
155:     def guard=(guard)
156:       @guard = guard
157:     end
158: 
159:     ##
160:     # Returns a transformer, if configured.
161:     #
162:     # Transformers can rewrite outgoing prompts and params before a request is
163:     # sent to the provider.
164:     #
165:     # @return [#call, nil]
166:     def transformer
167:       @transformer
168:     end
169: 
170:     ##
171:     # Sets a transformer.
172:     #
173:     # Transformers must implement `call(ctx, prompt, params)` and return a
174:     # two-element array of `[prompt, params]`.
175:     #
176:     # @param [#call, nil] transformer
177:     # @return [#call, nil]
178:     def transformer=(transformer)
179:       @transformer = transformer
180:     end
181: 
182:     # Interact with the context via the chat completions API.
183:     # This method immediately sends a request to the LLM and returns the response.
184:     #
185:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
186:     # @param params The params, including optional :role (defaults to :user), :stream, :tools, :schema etc.
187:     # @return [LLM::Response] Returns the LLM's response for this turn.
188:     # @example
189:     #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
190:     #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
191:     #   res = ctx.talk("Hello, what is your name?")
192:     #   puts res.messages[0].content
193:     def talk(prompt, params = {})
194:       @owner = @llm.request_owner
195:       compactor.compact!(prompt) if compactor.compact?(prompt)
196:       repair!(@messages, prompt)
197:       prompt, params, res = mode == :responses ? respond(prompt, params) : complete(prompt, params)
198:       self.compacted = false
199:       role = params[:role] || @llm.user_role
200:       role = @llm.tool_role if params[:role].nil? && [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
201:       @messages.concat LLM::Prompt === prompt ? prompt.to_a : [LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
202:       @messages.concat [res.choices[-1]]
203:       res
204:     end
205: 
206:     ##
207:     # Ask a question and return the content string directly.
208:     # Accepts `with:` for file attachments and a block for streaming.
209:     # This interface is compatible with RubyLLM's `ask` method.
210:     # @param [String] prompt
211:     # @param [Hash] options
212:     # @option options [String, Array<String>, nil] :with
213:     #  File path(s) to attach
214:     # @option options [#<<, LLM::Stream, nil] :stream
215:     #  A stream target
216:     # @yield [String] content chunks when streaming
217:     # @return [LLM::Response]
218:     def ask(prompt, options = {}, &block)
219:       options = {with: nil, stream: nil}.merge!(options || {})
220:       with, stream = options.values_at(:with, :stream)
221:       prompt = with ? [prompt, [*with].map { local_file(_1) }] : prompt
222:       target = if block
223:         blk = block.dup
224:         blk.singleton_class.alias_method(:<<, :call)
225:         blk
226:       else
227:         stream
228:       end
229:       target ? talk(prompt, stream: target) : talk(prompt)
230:     end
231: 
232:     ##
233:     # @return [String]
234:     def inspect
235:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} " \
236:       "@llm=#{@llm.class}, @mode=#{@mode.inspect}, @params=#{@params.inspect}, " \
237:       "@messages=#{@messages.inspect}>"
238:     end
239: 
240:     ##
241:     # Returns an array of functions that can be called
242:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function>]
243:     def functions
244:       return_ids = returns.map(&:id)
245:       @messages
246:         .select(&:assistant?)
247:         .flat_map do |msg|
248:           fns = msg.functions.select { _1.pending? && !return_ids.include?(_1.id) }
249:           fns.each do |fn|
250:             fn.tracer = tracer
251:             fn.model  = msg.model
252:           end
253:         end.extend(LLM::Function::Array)
254:     end
255: 
256:     ##
257:     # Returns whether there is pending tool work in this context.
258:     # This prefers queued streamed tool work when present, and otherwise
259:     # falls back to unresolved functions derived from the message history.
260:     # @return [Boolean]
261:     def functions?
262:       pending = queue
263:       (pending && !pending.empty?) || functions.any?
264:     end
265: 
266:     ##
267:     # Spawns a function through the context.
268:     #
269:     # When a guard is configured, this method can return an in-band guarded
270:     # tool error instead of spawning work.
271:     #
272:     # @param [LLM::Function] function
273:     # @param [Symbol] strategy
274:     # @return [LLM::Function::Return, LLM::Function::Task]
275:     def spawn(function, strategy)
276:       warning = guard&.call(self)
277:       return guarded_return_for(function, warning) if warning
278:       function.spawn(strategy)
279:     end
280: 
281:     ##
282:     # Returns tool returns accumulated in this context
283:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
284:     def returns
285:       @messages
286:         .select(&:tool_return?)
287:         .flat_map do |msg|
288:           LLM::Function::Return === msg.content ?
289:             [msg.content] :
290:             [*msg.content].grep(LLM::Function::Return)
291:         end
292:     end
293: 
294:     ##
295:     # Waits for queued tool work to finish.
296:     #
297:     # This prefers queued streamed tool work when the configured stream
298:     # exposes a non-empty queue. Otherwise it falls back to waiting on
299:     # the context's pending functions directly.
300:     #
301:     # @param [Symbol, Array<Symbol>] strategy
302:     #  If the stream queue already has tool work, `wait` will drain it
303:     #  without using this argument.
304:     #  Otherwise, this controls how pending functions are resolved directly.
305:     #  Use `:call` for sequential execution without spawning.
306:     # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] except
307:     #  A list of functions to exclude from the wait
308:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
309:     def wait(strategy, except: [])
310:       if LLM::Stream === stream && !stream.queue.empty?
311:         @queue = stream.queue
312:         @queue.wait
313:       else
314:         tools  = except.empty? ? functions : functions - except
315:         guards = guarded_returns(tools:)
316:         return guards if guards
317:         @queue = tools.spawn(strategy)
318:         returns = @queue.wait
319:         emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)
320:         returns
321:       end
322:     ensure
323:       @queue = nil
324:       @stream = nil
325:     end
326: 
327:     ##
328:     # Interrupt the active request, if any.
329:     # This is inspired by Go's context cancellation model.
330:     # @return [nil]
331:     def interrupt!
332:       pending = functions.to_a
333:       llm.interrupt!(@owner)
334:       queue&.interrupt!
335:       return if pending.empty?
336:       pending.each(&:interrupt!)
337:       returns = pending.map { _1.cancel(reason: "function call cancelled") }
338:       @messages << LLM::Message.new(@llm.tool_role, returns)
339:       nil
340:     end
341:     alias_method :cancel!, :interrupt!
342: 
343:     ##
344:     # Returns token usage accumulated in this context
345:     # @return [LLM::Object]
346:     def usage
347:       if usage = @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.usage
348:         LLM::Object.from(
349:           input_tokens: usage.input_tokens || 0,
350:           output_tokens: usage.output_tokens || 0,
351:           reasoning_tokens: usage.reasoning_tokens || 0,
352:           input_audio_tokens: usage.input_audio_tokens || 0,
353:           output_audio_tokens: usage.output_audio_tokens || 0,
354:           input_image_tokens: usage.input_image_tokens || 0,
355:           cache_read_tokens: usage.cache_read_tokens || 0,
356:           cache_write_tokens: usage.cache_write_tokens || 0,
357:           total_tokens: usage.total_tokens || 0
358:         )
359:       else
360:         ZERO_USAGE
361:       end
362:     end
363: 
364:     ##
365:     # Build a role-aware prompt for a single request.
366:     #
367:     # Prefer this method over {#build_prompt}. The older
368:     # method name is kept for backward compatibility.
369:     # @example
370:     #   prompt = ctx.prompt do
371:     #     system "Your task is to assist the user"
372:     #     user "Hello, can you assist me?"
373:     #   end
374:     #   ctx.talk(prompt)
375:     # @param [Proc] b
376:     #  A block that composes messages. If it takes one argument,
377:     #  it receives the prompt object. Otherwise it runs in prompt context.
378:     # @return [LLM::Prompt]
379:     def prompt(&b)
380:       LLM::Prompt.new(@llm, &b)
381:     end
382:     alias_method :build_prompt, :prompt
383: 
384:     ##
385:     # Recongize an object as a URL to an image
386:     # @param [String] url
387:     #  The URL
388:     # @return [LLM::Object]
389:     #  Returns a tagged object
390:     def image_url(url)
391:       LLM::Object.from(value: url, kind: :image_url)
392:     end
393: 
394:     ##
395:     # Recongize an object as a local file
396:     # @param [String] path
397:     #  The path
398:     # @return [LLM::Object]
399:     #  Returns a tagged object
400:     def local_file(path)
401:       LLM::Object.from(value: LLM.File(path), kind: :local_file)
402:     end
403: 
404:     ##
405:     # Reconginize an object as a remote file
406:     # @param [LLM::Response] res
407:     #  The response
408:     # @return [LLM::Object]
409:     #  Returns a tagged object
410:     def remote_file(res)
411:       LLM::Object.from(value: res, kind: :remote_file)
412:     end
413: 
414:     ##
415:     # @return [LLM::Tracer]
416:     #  Returns an LLM tracer
417:     def tracer
418:       @llm.tracer
419:     end
420: 
421:     ##
422:     # @return [LLM::Stream, #<<, nil]
423:     #  Returns a stream object, or nil
424:     def stream
425:       @stream || @params[:stream]
426:     end
427: 
428:     ##
429:     # Returns the model a Context is actively using
430:     # @return [String]
431:     def model
432:       messages.find(&:assistant?)&.model || @params[:model]
433:     end
434: 
435:     ##
436:     # @return [Hash]
437:     def to_h
438:       {
439:         schema_version: 1,
440:         model:,
441:         compacted:,
442:         messages: @messages.map { serialize_message(_1) }
443:       }
444:     end
445: 
446:     ##
447:     # @return [String]
448:     def to_json(...)
449:       LLM.json.dump(to_h, ...)
450:     end
451: 
452:     ##
453:     # Save the current context state
454:     # @example
455:     #  llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
456:     #  ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
457:     #  ctx.talk "Hello"
458:     #  ctx.save(path: "context.json")
459:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
460:     #  Might raise a number of SystemCallError subclasses
461:     # @return [void]
462:     def serialize(path:)
463:       ::File.binwrite path, LLM.json.dump(to_h)
464:     end
465:     alias_method :save, :serialize
466: 
467:     ##
468:     # @return [LLM::Cost]
469:     #  Returns an _approximate_ cost for a given context
470:     #  based on both the provider, and model
471:     def cost
472:       LLM::Cost.from(self)
473:     end
474: 
475:     ##
476:     # Returns the model's context window.
477:     # The context window is the maximum amount of input and output
478:     # tokens a model can consider in a single request.
479:     # @note
480:     #   This method returns 0 when the provider or
481:     #   model can't be found within {LLM::Registry}.
482:     # @return [Integer]
483:     def context_window
484:       LLM
485:         .registry_for(llm)
486:         .limit(model:)
487:         .context
488:     rescue LLM::NoSuchModelError, LLM::NoSuchRegistryError
489:       0
490:     end
491: 
492:     private
493: 
494:     ##
495:     # Binds runtime metadata onto an active stream.
496:     # @api private
497:     def bind!(stream, model, tools)
498:       return unless LLM::Stream === stream
499:       @stream = stream
500:       stream.extra[:ctx] = self
501:       stream.extra[:tracer] = tracer
502:       stream.extra[:model] = model
503:       stream.extra[:tools] = tools
504:     end
505: 
506:     ##
507:     # Returns the bound stream queue, if available.
508:     # @api private
509:     def queue
510:       [@queue, stream&.queue].compact.first
511:     rescue NoMethodError
512:       nil
513:     end
514: 
515:     ##
516:     # Loads skill directories and adapts them into tools.
517:     # @api private
518:     def load_skills(skills)
519:       [*skills].map { LLM::Skill.load(_1).to_tool(self) }
520:     end
521: 
522:     ##
523:     # Builds in-band guarded returns when the guard blocks tool work.
524:     # @api private
525:     def guarded_returns(tools:)
526:       warning = guard&.call(self)
527:       return unless warning
528:       tools.map { guarded_return_for(_1, warning) }
529:     end
530: 
531:     ##
532:     # Rewrites a prompt and params through the configured transformer.
533:     # @api private
534:     def transform(prompt, params)
535:       return [prompt, params] unless transformer
536:       stream = params[:stream]
537:       stream.on_transform(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
538:       transformer.call(self, prompt, params)
539:     ensure
540:       stream.on_transform_finish(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
541:     end
542: 
543:     ##
544:     # Executes a turn through the Responses API.
545:     # @api private
546:     def respond(prompt, params)
547:       params = @params.merge(params)
548:       prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
549:       bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
550:       res_id = params[:store] == false ? nil : @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.response&.response_id
551:       params = params.merge(previous_response_id: res_id, input: @messages.to_a).compact
552:       [prompt, params, @llm.responses.create(prompt, params)]
553:     end
554: 
555:     ##
556:     # Executes a turn through the chat completions API.
557:     # @api private
558:     def complete(prompt, params)
559:       params = params.merge(messages: @messages.to_a)
560:       params = @params.merge(params)
561:       prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
562:       bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
563:       [prompt, params, @llm.complete(prompt, params)]
564:     end
565: 
566:     ##
567:     # Builds one guarded tool return for a blocked function call.
568:     # @api private
569:     def guarded_return_for(function, warning)
570:       LLM::Function::Return.new(function.id, function.name, {
571:         error: true,
572:         type: LLM::GuardError.name,
573:         message: warning
574:       })
575:     end
576: 
577:     ##
578:     # Emits tool return callbacks for directly waited function work.
579:     # @api private
580:     def emit_tool_returns(tools, returns)
581:       return unless LLM::Stream === stream
582:       returns.each_with_index { |result, index| stream.on_tool_return(tools[index], result) }
583:     end
584: 
585:     ##
586:     # Closes assistant tool-call messages that do not have matching tool
587:     # responses. This can happen when a turn is interrupted while a tool
588:     # call is streaming or waiting for user confirmation.
589:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
590:     # @param [Object] prompt
591:     # @return [void]
592:     def repair!(messages, prompt)
593:       message = messages.last
594:       return unless message&.tool_call?
595:       returns = self.returns + [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return)
596:       cancelled = []
597:       [*message.extra.tool_calls].each do |tool|
598:         next if returns.any? { _1.id == tool[:id] }
599:         attrs = {cancelled: true, reason: "function call cancelled"}
600:         cancelled << LLM::Function::Return.new(tool.id, tool.name, attrs)
601:       end
602:       messages << LLM::Message.new(@llm.tool_role, cancelled) unless cancelled.empty?
603:     end
604:   end
605: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   class Message
5:     ##
6:     # Returns the role of the message
7:     # @return [Symbol]
8:     attr_reader :role
9: 
10:     ##
11:     # Returns the content of the message
12:     # @return [String]
13:     attr_reader :content
14: 
15:     ##
16:     # Returns extra context associated with the message
17:     # @return [Hash]
18:     attr_reader :extra
19: 
20:     ##
21:     # Returns a new message
22:     # @param [Symbol] role
23:     # @param [String] content
24:     # @param [Hash] extra
25:     # @return [LLM::Message]
26:     def initialize(role, content, extra = {})
27:       @role = role.to_s
28:       @content = content
29:       @extra = LLM::Object.from(extra)
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Returns a Hash representation of the message.
34:     # @return [Hash]
35:     def to_h
36:       {
37:         role:,
38:         content:,
39:         reasoning_content:,
40:         compaction: extra.compaction,
41:         tools: extra.tool_calls&.map { LLM::Object === _1 ? _1.to_h : _1 },
42:         usage:,
43:         original_tool_calls: extra.original_tool_calls
44:       }.compact.then { preserve_nil_content(_1) }
45:     end
46: 
47:     ##
48:     # @return [String]
49:     def to_json(...)
50:       LLM.json.dump(to_h, ...)
51:     end
52: 
53:     ##
54:     # Returns true when two objects have the same role and content
55:     # @param [Object] other
56:     #  The other object to compare
57:     # @return [Boolean]
58:     def ==(other)
59:       if other.respond_to?(:to_h)
60:         to_h == other.to_h
61:       else
62:         false
63:       end
64:     end
65:     alias_method :eql?, :==
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # Try to parse JSON content
69:     # @return [Hash]
70:     #  Returns the parsed content as a Hash
71:     def content!
72:       LLM.json.load(content)
73:     end
74: 
75:     ##
76:     # Returns reasoning content associated with the message
77:     # @return [String, nil]
78:     def reasoning_content
79:       extra.reasoning_content
80:     end
81: 
82:     ##
83:     # Returns true when a message was created by context compaction
84:     # @return [Boolean]
85:     def compaction?
86:       !!extra.compaction
87:     end
88: 
89:     ##
90:     # Returns true when a message contains an image URL
91:     # @return [Boolean]
92:     def image_url?
93:       image_urls.any?
94:     end
95: 
96:     ##
97:     # Returns image URL content items from the message
98:     # @return [Array<LLM::Object>]
99:     def image_urls
100:       content_items.select { LLM::Object === _1 && _1.kind == :image_url }
101:     end
102: 
103:     ##
104:     # Returns true when a message contains a local or remote file
105:     # @return [Boolean]
106:     def file?
107:       files.any?
108:     end
109: 
110:     ##
111:     # Returns local and remote file content items from the message
112:     # @return [Array<LLM::Object>]
113:     def files
114:       content_items.select do
115:         LLM::Object === _1 && [:local_file, :remote_file].include?(_1.kind)
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     ##
120:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function>]
121:     def functions
122:       @functions ||= tool_calls.filter_map do |fn|
123:         function = available_tools.find { _1.name.to_s == fn["name"] } || next
124:         function = function.dup
125:         function.tap { _1.id = fn.id }
126:         function.tap { _1.arguments = fn.arguments }
127:       end
128:     end
129: 
130:     ##
131:     # Returns true when the message is an assistant message
132:     # @return [Boolean]
133:     def assistant?
134:       role == "assistant" || role == "model"
135:     end
136: 
137:     ##
138:     # Returns true when the message is a system message
139:     # @return [Boolean]
140:     def system?
141:       role == "system"
142:     end
143: 
144:     ##
145:     # Returns true when the message is a user message
146:     # @return [Boolean]
147:     def user?
148:       role == "user"
149:     end
150: 
151:     ##
152:     # @return [Boolean]
153:     #  Returns true when the message requests a function call
154:     def tool_call?
155:       tool_calls.any?
156:     end
157: 
158:     ##
159:     # @return [Boolean]
160:     #  Returns true when the message represents a function return
161:     def tool_return?
162:       LLM::Function::Return === content ||
163:         [*content].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
164:     end
165: 
166:     ##
167:     # @note
168:     #  This method returns a response for assistant messages,
169:     #  and it returns nil for non-assistant messages
170:     # @return [LLM::Response, nil]
171:     #  Returns the response associated with the message, or nil
172:     def response
173:       extra.response
174:     end
175: 
176:     ##
177:     # @note
178:     #  This method might return annotations for assistant messages,
179:     #  and it returns an empty array for non-assistant messages
180:     # Returns annotations associated with the message
181:     # @return [Array<LLM::Object>]
182:     def annotations
183:       @annotations ||= LLM::Object.from(extra.annotations || [])
184:     end
185: 
186:     ##
187:     # @note
188:     #  This method returns token usage for assistant messages,
189:     #  and it returns nil for non-assistant messages
190:     # Returns token usage statistics
191:     # @return [LLM::Object, nil]
192:     def usage
193:       @usage ||= extra.usage || response&.usage
194:     end
195:     alias_method :token_usage, :usage
196: 
197:     ##
198:     # @return [String, nil]
199:     #  Returns the model associated with a message
200:     def model
201:       response&.model
202:     end
203: 
204:     ##
205:     # Returns a string representation of the message
206:     # @return [String]
207:     def inspect
208:       "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} " \
209:       "tool_call=#{tool_calls.any?} role=#{role.inspect} " \
210:       "content=#{content.inspect} reasoning_content=#{reasoning_content.inspect}>"
211:     end
212: 
213:     private
214: 
215:     def preserve_nil_content(hash)
216:       hash[:content] = content if content.nil?
217:       hash
218:     end
219: 
220:     def tool_calls
221:       @tool_calls ||= LLM::Object.from(extra.tool_calls || [])
222:     end
223: 
224:     def available_tools
225:       tools = extra.tools || response&.__tools__ || []
226:       tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:function) ? _1.function : _1 }
227:     end
228: 
229:     def content_items
230:       Array(content)
231:     end
232:   end
233: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
11:     #  The messages to adapt
12:     # @return [Hash]
13:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
14:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
15:       messages.each do |message|
16:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
17:         next if adapted.nil?
18:         if system?(message)
19:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
20:         else
21:           payload[:messages] << adapted
22:         end
23:       end
24:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
25:       payload
26:     end
27: 
28:     private
29: 
30:     ##
31:     # @param [Hash] params
32:     # @return [Hash]
33:     def adapt_tools(tools)
34:       return {} unless tools&.any?
35:       {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
36:     end
37: 
38:     def system?(message)
39:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
40:         message.system?
41:       else
42:         Hash === message and message[:role].to_s == "system"
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end
46: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Anthropic::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the Anthropic chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         else
22:           adapt_message
23:         end
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     private
28: 
29:     def adapt_message
30:       if message.tool_call?
31:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_tool_calls}
32:       else
33:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
34:       end
35:     end
36: 
37:     def adapt_tool_calls
38:       message.extra[:tool_calls].filter_map do |tool|
39:         next unless tool[:id] && tool[:name]
40:         {type: "tool_use", id: tool[:id], name: tool[:name], input: LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool[:arguments])}
41:       end
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # @param [String, URI] content
46:     #  The content to format
47:     # @return [String, Hash]
48:     #  The formatted content
49:     def adapt_content(content)
50:       case content
51:       when Hash
52:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : [content]
53:       when Array
54:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
55:       when LLM::Object
56:         adapt_object(content)
57:       when String
58:         [{type: :text, text: content}]
59:       when LLM::Response
60:         adapt_remote_file(content)
61:       when LLM::Message
62:         adapt_content(content.content)
63:       when LLM::Function::Return
64:         [{type: "tool_result", tool_use_id: content.id, content: [{type: :text, text: LLM.json.dump(content.value)}]}]
65:       else
66:         prompt_error!(content)
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     def adapt_object(object)
71:       case object.kind
72:       when :image_url
73:         [{type: :image, source: {type: "url", url: object.value.to_s}}]
74:       when :local_file
75:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
76:       when :remote_file
77:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
78:       else
79:         prompt_error!(content)
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def adapt_local_file(file)
84:       if file.image?
85:         [{type: :image, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
86:       elsif file.pdf?
87:         [{type: :document, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
88:       else
89:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{file.class}) " \
90:                                 "is not an image or PDF, and therefore not supported by the " \
91:                                 "Anthropic API"
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
96:       prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
97:       [{type: file.file_type, source: {type: :file, file_id: file.id}}]
98:     end
99: 
100:     def prompt_error!(content)
101:       if LLM::Object === content
102:       else
103:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
104:                                 "is not supported by the Anthropic API."
105:       end
106:     end
107: 
108:     def message = @message
109:     def content = message.content
110:   end
111: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module ResponseAdapter
7:     require_relative "response_adapter/completion"
8:     require_relative "response_adapter/enumerable"
9:     require_relative "response_adapter/file"
10:     require_relative "response_adapter/models"
11:     require_relative "response_adapter/web_search"
12: 
13:     module_function
14: 
15:     ##
16:     # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
17:     # @param [Symbol] type
18:     # @return [LLM::Response]
19:     def adapt(res, type:)
20:       response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
21:       response.extend(select(type))
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @api private
26:     def select(type)
27:       case type
28:       when :completion then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::Completion
29:       when :enumerable then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::Enumerable
30:       when :file then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::File
31:       when :models then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::Models
32:       when :web_search then LLM::Anthropic::ResponseAdapter::WebSearch
33:       else
34:         raise ArgumentError, "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
35:       end
36:     end
37:   end
38: end

99:       source = texts.empty? && tools.any? ? [{"text" => ""}] : texts
100:       source.map.with_index do |choice, index|
101:         extra = {
102:           index:, response: self,
103:           tool_calls: adapt_tool_calls(tools), original_tool_calls: tools
104:         }
105:         LLM::Message.new(role, choice["text"], extra)
106:       end
107:     end

111:         {
112:           id: tool.id,
113:           name: tool.name,
114:           arguments: LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool.input)
115:         }
116:       end

120:     def texts = @texts ||= parts.select { _1["type"] == "text" }
121:     def tools = @tools ||= parts.select { _1["type"] == "tool_use" }

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
20:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
21:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @param [Hash] chunk
26:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
27:     def parse!(chunk)
28:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
33:     # @return [void]
34:     def free
35:     end
36: 
37:     private
38: 
39:     def merge!(chunk)
40:       if chunk["type"] == "message_start"
41:         merge_message!(chunk["message"])
42:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_start"
43:         @body["content"][chunk["index"]] = chunk["content_block"]
44:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_delta"
45:         if chunk["delta"]["type"] == "text_delta"
46:           @body["content"][chunk["index"]]["text"] << chunk["delta"]["text"]
47:           emit_content(chunk["delta"]["text"])
48:         elsif chunk["delta"]["type"] == "input_json_delta"
49:           content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
50:           if Hash === content["input"]
51:             content["input"] = chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
52:           else
53:             content["input"] ||= +""
54:             content["input"] << chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
55:           end
56:         end
57:       elsif chunk["type"] == "message_delta"
58:         merge_message!(chunk["delta"]) if chunk["delta"]
59:         extras = chunk.reject { |k, _| k == "type" || k == "delta" }
60:         merge_message!(extras) unless extras.empty?
61:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_stop"
62:         content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
63:         if content["input"]
64:           content["input"] = LLM.json.load(content["input"])
65:         end
66:         if content["type"] == "tool_use"
67:           emit_tool(content)
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_message!(message)
73:       message.each_pair do |key, value|
74:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair)
75:           @body[key] ||= {}
76:           deep_merge!(@body[key], value)
77:         else
78:           @body[key] = value
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def deep_merge!(target, source)
84:       source.each_pair do |key, value|
85:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair) && target[key].respond_to?(:each_pair)
86:           deep_merge!(target[key], value)
87:         else
88:           target[key] = value
89:         end
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     def emit_content(value)
94:       if @can_emit_content
95:         @stream.on_content(value)
96:       elsif @can_push_content
97:         @stream << value
98:       end
99:     end
100: 
101:     def emit_tool(tool)
102:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
103:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
104:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
105:     end
106: 
107:     def resolve_tool(tool)
108:       registered = @stream.__find__(tool["name"])
109:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(tool["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
110:         fn.id = tool["id"]
111:         fn.arguments = LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool["input"])
112:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
113:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
114:       end
115:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [LLM::Error]
45:     def error
46:       if res.server_error?
47:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
48:       elsif res.unauthorized?
49:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
50:       elsif res.rate_limited?
51:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
52:       elsif res.not_found?
53:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       else
55:         LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
56:       end
57:     end
58:   end
59: end

3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
11:     #  The messages to adapt
12:     # @return [Array<Hash>]
13:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
14:       messages.filter_map do |message|
15:         Completion.new(message).adapt
16:       end
17:     end
18: 
19:     private
20: 
21:     ##
22:     # @param [Hash] params
23:     # @return [Hash]
24:     def adapt_schema(params)
25:       return {} unless params and params[:schema]
26:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
27:       schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
28:       {generationConfig: {response_mime_type: "application/json", response_schema: schema}}
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @param [Hash] params
33:     # @return [Hash]
34:     def adapt_tools(tools)
35:       return {} unless tools&.any?
36:       platform, functions = [tools.grep(LLM::ServerTool), tools.grep(LLM::Function)]
37:       {tools: [*platform, {functionDeclarations: functions.map { _1.adapt(self) }}]}
38:     end
39:   end
40: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Google::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the Gemini chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], parts: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         elsif message.tool_call?
22:           {role: message.role, parts: message.extra.original_tool_calls}
23:         else
24:           {role: message.role, parts: adapt_content(message.content)}
25:         end
26:       end
27:     end
28: 
29:     def adapt_content(content)
30:       case content
31:       when Array
32:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
33:       when String
34:         [{text: content}]
35:       when LLM::Response
36:         adapt_remote_file(content)
37:       when LLM::Message
38:         adapt_content(content.content)
39:       when LLM::Function::Return
40:         [{functionResponse: {name: content.name, response: adapt_function_response(content.value)}}]
41:       when LLM::Object
42:         adapt_object(content)
43:       else
44:         prompt_error!(content)
45:       end
46:     end
47: 
48:     def adapt_object(object)
49:       case object.kind
50:       when :image_url
51:         [{file_data: {file_uri: object.value.to_s}}]
52:       when :local_file
53:         file = object.value
54:         [{inline_data: {mime_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
55:       when :remote_file
56:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
57:       else
58:         prompt_error!(object)
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
63:       return prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
64:       [{file_data: {mime_type: file.mime_type, file_uri: file.uri}}]
65:     end
66: 
67:     def adapt_function_response(value)
68:       Hash === value ? value : {result: value}
69:     end
70: 
71:     def prompt_error!(object)
72:       if LLM::Object === object
73:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
74:                                 "supported by the Gemini API"
75:       else
76:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
77:                                 "is not supported by the Gemini API"
78:       end
79:     end
80: 
81:     def message = @message
82:     def content = message.content
83:   end
84: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Google::ResponseAdapter
4:   module Completion
5:     ##
6:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#messages)
7:     def messages
8:       adapt_choices
9:     end
10:     alias_method :choices, :messages
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#id)
14:     def id
15:       body["responseId"]
16:     end
17: 
18:     ##
19:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_tokens)
20:     def input_tokens
21:       body.usageMetadata.promptTokenCount || 0
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_tokens)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"candidates" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: []}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
22:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
23:     end
24: 
25:     ##
26:     # @param [Hash] chunk
27:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
28:     def parse!(chunk)
29:       tap { merge_chunk!(chunk) }
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
34:     # @return [void]
35:     def free
36:       @emits.clear
37:     end
38: 
39:     private
40: 
41:     def merge_chunk!(chunk)
42:       chunk.each do |key, value|
43:         k = key.to_s
44:         if k == "candidates"
45:           merge_candidates!(value)
46:         elsif k == "usageMetadata" &&
47:             @body["usageMetadata"].is_a?(Hash) &&
48:             value.is_a?(Hash)
49:           @body["usageMetadata"] = @body["usageMetadata"].merge(value)
50:         else
51:           @body[k] = value
52:         end
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:     def merge_candidates!(deltas)
57:       deltas.each do |delta|
58:         index = delta["index"].to_i
59:         @body["candidates"][index] ||= {"content" => {"parts" => []}}
60:         candidate = @body["candidates"][index]
61:         delta.each do |key, value|
62:           k = key.to_s
63:           if k == "content"
64:             merge_candidate_content!(candidate["content"], value, index) if value
65:           else
66:             candidate[k] = value # Overwrite other fields
67:           end
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_candidate_content!(content, delta, cindex)
73:       delta.each do |key, value|
74:         k = key.to_s
75:         if k == "parts"
76:           content["parts"] ||= []
77:           merge_content_parts!(content["parts"], value, cindex) if value
78:         else
79:           content[k] = value
80:         end
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def merge_content_parts!(parts, deltas, cindex)
85:       deltas.each do |delta|
86:         if delta["text"]
87:           merge_text!(parts, delta)
88:         elsif delta["functionCall"]
89:           merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
90:         elsif delta["inlineData"]
91:           parts << delta
92:         elsif delta["functionResponse"]
93:           parts << delta
94:         elsif delta["fileData"]
95:           parts << delta
96:         else
97:           parts << delta
98:         end
99:       end
100:     end
101: 
102:     def merge_text!(parts, delta)
103:       last_existing_part = parts.last
104:       text = delta["text"]
105:       if last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) && last_existing_part["text"]
106:         last_existing_part["text"] ||= +""
107:         last_existing_part["text"] << text
108:         emit_content(text)
109:       else
110:         parts << delta
111:         emit_content(text)
112:       end
113:     end
114: 
115:     def merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
116:       last_existing_part = parts.last
117:       last_call = last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) ? last_existing_part["functionCall"] : nil
118:       delta_call = delta["functionCall"]
119:       if last_call.is_a?(Hash) && delta_call.is_a?(Hash)
120:         last_existing_part["functionCall"] = last_call.merge(delta_call)
121:         delta.each do |key, value|
122:           next if key == "functionCall"
123:           last_existing_part[key] = value
124:         end
125:       else
126:         parts << delta
127:       end
128:       emit_tool(parts.length - 1, cindex, parts.last || delta)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def emit_content(value)
132:       if @can_emit_content
133:         @stream.on_content(value)
134:       elsif @can_push_content
135:         @stream << value
136:       end
137:     end
138: 
139:     def emit_tool(pindex, cindex, part)
140:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
141:       return unless complete_tool?(part)
142:       key = [cindex, pindex]
143:       return if @emits[:tools].include?(key)
144:       function, error = resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
145:       @emits[:tools] << key
146:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def complete_tool?(part)
150:       call = part["functionCall"]
151:       call && call["name"] && Hash === call["args"]
152:     end
153: 
154:     def resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
155:       call = part["functionCall"]
156:       registered = @stream.__find__(call["name"])
157:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(call["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
158:         fn.id = LLM::Google.tool_id(part:, cindex:, pindex:)
159:         fn.arguments = call["args"]
160:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
161:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
162:       end
163:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
164:     end
165:   end
166: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::Google::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.bad_request?
55:         reason = body.dig("error", "details", 0, "reason")
56:         if reason == "API_KEY_INVALID"
57:           LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
58:         else
59:           LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
60:         end
61:       elsif res.rate_limited?
62:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
63:       elsif res.not_found?
64:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
65:       else
66:         LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
72:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
73:     def parse_body!
74:       if String === res.body
75:         LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
76:       elsif Hash === res.body
77:         LLM::Object.from(res.body)
78:       else
79:         res.body
80:       end
81:     rescue
82:       res.body
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Ollama::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the Ollama chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role]}.merge(adapt_content(message[:content]))
21:         else
22:           adapt_message
23:         end
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     private
28: 
29:     def adapt_content(content)
30:       case content
31:       when String
32:         {content:}
33:       when LLM::Message
34:         adapt_content(content.content)
35:       when LLM::Function::Return
36:         throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
37:       when LLM::Object
38:         adapt_object(content)
39:       else
40:         prompt_error!(content)
41:       end
42:     end
43: 
44:     def adapt_message
45:       case content
46:       when Array
47:         adapt_array
48:       else
49:         {role: message.role}.merge(adapt_content(content))
50:       end
51:     end
52: 
53:     def adapt_array
54:       if content.empty?
55:         nil
56:       elsif returns.any?
57:         returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
58:       else
59:         content.flat_map { {role: message.role}.merge(adapt_content(_1)) }
60:       end
61:     end
62: 
63:     def adapt_object(object)
64:       case object.kind
65:       when :local_file then adapt_local_file(object.value)
66:       when :remote_file then prompt_error!(object)
67:       when :image_url then prompt_error!(object)
68:       else prompt_error!(object)
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def adapt_local_file(file)
73:       if file.image?
74:         {content: "This message has an image associated with it", images: [file.to_b64]}
75:       else
76:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given local file (an instance of #{file.class}) " \
77:                                 "is not an image, and therefore not supported by the " \
78:                                 "Ollama API"
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     def prompt_error!(object)
83:       if LLM::Object === object
84:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
85:                                 "supported by the Ollama API"
86:       else
87:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{object.class}) " \
88:                                 "is not supported by the Ollama API"
89:       end
90:     end
91: 
92:     def message = @message
93:     def content = message.content
94:     def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
95:   end
96: end

3: class LLM::Ollama
4:   ##
